📚 Forums / firmware-development / FW Request: RAK WisMesh 1W Booster (3401 + 13302)
TF-Mambo 2026-01-17 7:40 a.m.
We have a new player on the block. "The WisMesh 1W Booster Starter Kit brings more TX power and better reception to Meshtastic networks. Powered by the RAK3401 WisBlock Core module with the Nordic nRF52840 MCU and a RAK13302 1W LoRa transceiver module, mounted on a RAK19007 Base Board." I see MT has the FW already.TIA

Cisien 2026-01-18 10:07 p.m.
this supposedly has a filter, but the noise floor is much worse than an unfiltered 1w pi hat with an antenna on the roof
10:08 p.m.
the 5v line draws 750mw when transmitting
10:09 p.m.
1 amp if you don't have usb plugged in

Cisien 2026-01-18 10:27 p.m.

1
10:28 p.m.
to the extent that my tinysa and nanovna are calibrated
ThatGuy 2026-01-18 10:46 p.m.
I was trying to figure out the other night how to accurately calibrate my nanovna on this hardware.
Cisien 2026-01-18 11:14 p.m.
do the full calibration with a pigtail, use that same pigtail to do the thru test, and use that pigtail when connecting to your 40db enumerator and an extra pigtail. look at your S21 logmag reading. that's the total attenuation of your attenuator and pigtail. Then use the attenuator and 2nd pigtail on a spectrum analyzer, set the external gain to <the s21 logmag reading> (-41.2), then trigger the node to transmit (advert from the serial console for example). watch what the tinysa reports, that's your output power
1
11:14 p.m.
give or take a db or two
Hunter 2026-01-18 11:34 p.m.
Make sure to use the attenuator. I would say 40dB min, 50 would probably be best. You want the input to be around -25 dBm or less.
Cisien 2026-01-18 11:35 p.m.
i measured my attenuator stack at 61db
Fokai 2026-01-18 11:47 p.m.
Am I reading correctly in the specsheet that we set the tx to 22 to get the ~30db out?
Cisien 2026-01-18 11:47 p.m.
yes

Craig VK2BTQ 2026-01-23 5:29 a.m.

2
Craig VK2BTQ
Is there a table like the Heltec v4 to show what you need to set the SX1262 to to get the particular dBm outputs?
TF-Mambo 2026-01-23 9:38 p.m.
The only thing I am able to find at the moment is the data sheet for the SKY66122. https://www.skyworksinc.com/-/media/SkyWorks/Documents/Products/2901-3000/SKY66122-11_204782G.pdf
Craig VK2BTQ 2026-01-23 9:41 p.m.
Tnx. I was looking at it but just couldn't find the tx input to output gains, have I missed it somewhere?
TF-Mambo 2026-01-23 9:44 p.m.
What I can gather from this chart is the Pin would be your TX setting and the Pout would be what is added. Per the chart looks linear which is great but it rolls off at one point. Won't know more until I get my hands on mine and measure the TX out at various levels.
Craig VK2BTQ 2026-01-23 10:01 p.m.
tnx for that.
TF-Mambo 2026-01-27 2:27 p.m.
OK. Just got my order for the RAK 1W. Let's see how this adventure goes.
TF-Mambo
OK. Just got my order for the RAK 1W. Let's see how this adventure goes.
ThatGuy 2026-01-27 2:59 p.m.
So far, I'm finding the 1W is much more susceptible to background noise. I'm going to order a different solar management board and see if that helps my situation. I deployed my 1W to a site on Friday where I had been getting good service from a Rak4631 node, and it performed horribly. To the point of being unusable and unreachable. Literally swapped out the boards, and added the power pins for the 5v input to the header pins on my power board, and no other changes. Same battery, same solar board, same antenna. Ended up going back at the end of the day to put the 4631 back in the so I would have connectivity during the ice storm. The .250W board went right back to working great. Getting 16.5 mile connectivity tall building to tower range. Couldn't see the tower at all with the 1W board. I'm underimpressed so far. (edited)
ThatGuy
So far, I'm finding the 1W is much more susceptible to background noise. I'm going to order a different solar management board and see if that helps my situation. Literally swapped out the boards, and added the power pins for the 5v input to the header pins on my power board, and no other changes. Same battery, same solar board, same antenna. Ended up going back at the end of the day to put the 4631 back in the so I would have connectivity during the ice storm. The .250W board went right back to working great. Getting 16.5 mile connectivity tall building to tower range. Couldn't see the tower at all with the 1W board. I'm underimpressed so far. (edited)

Fascinating information. Thanks for sharing. I am testing TX at the moment then will test RX as best as I can. I will post any findings.
TF-Mambo
Fascinating information. Thanks for sharing. I am testing TX at the moment then will test RX as best as I can. I will post any findings.
ThatGuy 2026-01-27 3:14 p.m.
Admittedly, it's not a great location noise wise. Near a lot of power distribution and HVAC stuff, but it was what I could get to at the location without climbing gear. This is a test location for us right now. The node will eventually be mounted about 20 feet up on a sign structure that is away from most of that electrical gear. I was really surprised at the performance drop though. I didn't expect that at all. If you don't mind, can you share your power setup for the 1W?
TF-Mambo 2026-01-27 3:17 p.m.
Yea that would introduce noise. Right it is just on a bench. Once I am convinced on the power and advantages, I will design a case and make it EDC ready.

4:08 p.m.
Also, measured the voltage on the RAK13302 and it is getting 5V with the 3.7V battery. I am currently testing with the 3000mAh battery.
1
4:10 p.m.
Very odd. Have to keep testing to see why we are not getting 1W.
ThatGuy 2026-01-27 4:13 p.m.
I had mine powered as follows: DFROBOT v1.1 Solar Power Management board: 3000mAh flatpack battery hooked to DFRobot for higher amperage charging. USB pigtail from DFRobot to JPT solar pigtail into solar input on Rak 19007 baseboard 5v regulated header output on DFRobot to external power input on Rak13302 module, with jumper in EXT config.
4:14 p.m.
In that power config, I was getting a +6db gain to distant repeaters over what I was getting from the nearby 4631 node. This would be roughly a quadrupling of power, I believe, which should have been the difference between the 4631 and the 1W.
TF-Mambo 2026-01-27 4:16 p.m.
I have to do more testing to see. I am going to flash an old 4631 I have to see if it still works and compare.

Cisien 2026-01-27 10:12 p.m.
pretty much all amplified nodes will have that. there's a filter on this board, so it's less likely to have problems with cellular/paging. The noise floor will be higher on amplified nodes, that's normal. It's actually concerning if the noise floor is really low (-120)
Cisien
pretty much all amplified nodes will have that. there's a filter on this board, so it's less likely to have problems with cellular/paging. The noise floor will be higher on amplified nodes, that's normal. It's actually concerning if the noise floor is really low (-120)
ThatGuy 2026-01-27 10:12 p.m.
Good to know. Thank you.
10:13 p.m.
When deployed at the remote site, it was bouncing from -104 to -74 and all points in between at each refresh.
Cisien 2026-01-27 10:13 p.m.
yeah, that needs more filtering
ThatGuy 2026-01-27 10:13 p.m.
At my house, it was running steadily at -111 to -108. (edited)
Cisien 2026-01-27 10:14 p.m.
i'm not quite sure yet, but you might have to be careful to use exact 1/2 wavelengths of coax (counting velocity factor) when adding an external filter

and sometimes as low as -114
Cisien 2026-01-27 10:14 p.m.
it may not matter because the rak has a saw filter, instead of cavity

ThatGuy 2026-01-28 3:01 p.m.
Figured it out (I think). Commit 4f46ec7 removed NRF52BoardOTA and incorporated into into the base NRF52Board. The RAK3401Board.h header still calls for NRF52BoardOTA. It looks like recrof has a PR in line for merging to fix it here: https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/pull/1507/changes/d5a73b239437ed6a1c9f9512246169e8238b25c8
fix: build errors because of changes in RAK3401Board base class by ...
+cleanup

ThatGuy 2026-01-28 5:13 p.m.
Turns out, there were two PRs 1507 and 1508.

1
Ed 2026-02-08 11:51 a.m.
What I'm hoping one of you folks will find out is how the new RAK board with it's built in rx filter receives compared to the other viable option of a Heltec V4 with the ceramic filter added. If the RAK performs as well I'll just buy it and try it but if not I guess I'll buy my first Heltec.
Fokai 2026-02-08 9:42 p.m.
I only had the rak 1w up at the very first iteration of firmware and it fell behind the Heltec V4 with a ceramic filter by about 20% of packets received at my specific location. I now run a V4 with LNA bypass no filter and I'm very happy. I'd like to compare it again to the Rak 1w, but haven't found the time. V4 is darn good for the price and performance, but maybe the rak is better long term as the firmware gets better?
paul / BostonMesh 2026-02-09 4:18 p.m.
Are others encountering RX issues with the 3401/1W booster? I was running a stationG2 and 1W booster in my yard and disabled the repeat function on the G2. I’m also running meshcore-bot and noticed that the bot is missing commands sent it that are being heard by a nearby companion. In all cases, the 1W booster did not repeat the packet. I’m also seeing cases where the bot responds to a command but the packet was never repeated back out. I’m also running an observer and seeing that the bot successfully TX’d but the booster did not repeat it back out the mesh at large.
4:20 p.m.
I’ve been running the nightly from last Friday with the improved NRF powersaving code. Prior to that, the first 1.12 nightly for the 3401.
4:20 p.m.
One other person in the region is also reporting Rx issues with the 3401.
andyshinn
i don't have the radio stable on current dev branch while running on just EX_5V. do y'all have it running stable on just EX_5V?
ThatGuy 2026-02-09 5:20 p.m.
I finally got things stable by using USB and EX_5V. I tried a couple of other configs and couldn't get stable performance I was happy with.
paul / BostonMesh
One other person in the region is also reporting Rx issues with the 3401.
ThatGuy 2026-02-09 5:37 p.m.
I have noticed that the noise floor is consistently about 10db higher on the 1W than it is on the 4631/19007 standard kit. I have one of each up in trees about 20 yards apart in my yard. The 4631 usually runs around -114 and the 3402/1W around -104->-100
paul / BostonMesh 2026-02-09 5:44 p.m.
I just flashed the latest nightly. It didn’t repeat my first test message but once it “woke up” it’s been fine.
1
ThatGuy 2026-02-09 5:47 p.m.
I don't know why it happens, but I have caught myself thinking that it seems to take a few minutes to "settle in" and get stable.
paul / BostonMesh 2026-02-09 6:31 p.m.
There definitely seems to be a sleep-like issue with this board. When it is awake it is fine.
TF-Mambo 2026-02-11 7:02 p.m.
Try the "set agc.refresh.interval 4" command on the repeater and see if that helps.
paul / BostonMesh 2026-02-12 8:39 a.m.
I tried that last night - not seeing much change.
8:40 a.m.
A local member has been reviewing how meshtastic implements the firmware for the 3401 and has noticed some differences. He has an updated firmware that he is testing out.

TF-Mambo 2026-02-12 2:17 p.m.

andyshinn 2026-02-12 2:36 p.m.
i'm curious if this is what i was hitting as well. were you able to see the LEDs (the charge LED) on the board when it stopped responding? did you notice if it was blinking differently at all?
2:37 p.m.
i think in my case it is a MCU brownout issue because i added some code to blink the blue LED and it eventually stops blinking and never recovers. here is the patch if anyone cares to test with a heartbeat LED on repeater as well: https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/pull/1667
Separate out StatusLED so it can be used for repeater by andyshinn ...
Separates out the StatusLED to its own class so it can be used with repeaters for testing.

Hunter 2026-02-13 11:31 p.m.
that makes sense, and is full power. that tx setting is for the sx1262 radio. The amplifier is separate, and not controllable.
11:33 p.m.
going higher than 22dbm actually has a chance of burning stuff up and doing damage

going higher than 22dbm actually has a chance of burning stuff up and doing damage

realtag 2026-02-14 7:20 p.m.
I've been noticing that my 1 watt 3401 amplifier is having issues when the voltage on battery dips down below certain point
7:21 p.m.
I opened an "issue" on github https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/issues/1697#issuecomment-3902618765
RAK WisMesh 1W Booster (3401 + 13302) amplifier on solar power · ...
Hi everyone, So I am would like to discuss my experience using RAK WisMesh 1W Booster (3401 + 13302) powered from solar power. For the most part it works fine from batteries alone, but then the pro...
7:22 p.m.
basically I found that you can set TX lower and then TX works again but it can still happen even with lower TX power. plugging in solar or powerbank instantly fixes the issue
7:22 p.m.
when amp is not working, the tx range drops down to just a few meters
andyshinn 2026-02-14 9:28 p.m.
are you still able to communicate with it even when the power output is that low (for example, from another companion in the same room)?
andyshinn
are you still able to communicate with it even when the power output is that low (for example, from another companion in the same room)?
realtag 2026-02-14 10:40 p.m.
yeah of course. The amplifier still works just at much lower power, I set it to transmit at 17dBm the lowest, can probably go even lower.
TF-Mambo 2026-02-15 12:24 p.m.
Updated to 1.13 and looks like NF is now working. Averaging -97. (edited)

ThatGuy 2026-02-18 12:02 p.m.
Is anyone getting stable production-ready results on the 3401? I had one working (or so I thought) at my house up in a tree. Took the box, mounted it on a frame, and took it to the site. I'm getting substantially worse performance from it than I was the 4631 test node I had at the site before. I basically swapped the two nodes. Took the 4631 home and put it back up in the same tree where the 1W was before. Now, I can't reach the downtown location which was no problem before the swapout. Put a 1W up in the tree at home, and STILL can't hit the downtown. Anyone have any thoughts? I'm about ready to call these crap and just go back to the 4631s.

Seems like there are issues with the RAK3401 still on 1.13. I have a fully loaded unit with BME680, Buzzer, RTC, and GPS. Everything seems to work except the GPS. Also, The unit is extremely overheating.

TF-Mambo
Try 1.12 and see what happens. For me, they are both still overheating. Going to play with the source and build my own FW to see if I can figure out what is going on.
ThatGuy 2026-02-18 12:24 p.m.
Let me know what you find.
12:25 p.m.
Please
andyshinn 2026-02-18 12:31 p.m.
it definitely has more options for powering and other variables. for troubleshooting i think it is helpful to know the radio power path, antenna pigtail, antenna, etc. just based on previous experience alone on the higher power radios i would estimate that the 13302 is slightly more deaf than the 4631 (which is the "gold standard" at receiving in my experience). so, more messages may be getting to their destination. but the return ack may be more unreliable i've got mine now running for more than 48 hours on the ground after swapping out to a waveshare solar board since it claims 3A on the 5V output where most others only seem to be 1A. the RAK docs call out needing 1.5A on EX_5V. so, i imagine there are going to be power concerns that people run into a bunch
andyshinn
it definitely has more options for powering and other variables. for troubleshooting i think it is helpful to know the radio power path, antenna pigtail, antenna, etc. just based on previous experience alone on the higher power radios i would estimate that the 13302 is slightly more deaf than the 4631 (which is the "gold standard" at receiving in my experience). so, more messages may be getting to their destination. but the return ack may be more unreliable i've got mine now running for more than 48 hours on the ground after swapping out to a waveshare solar board since it claims 3A on the 5V output where most others only seem to be 1A. the RAK docs call out needing 1.5A on EX_5V. so, i imagine there are going to be power concerns that people run into a bunch
ThatGuy 2026-02-18 12:56 p.m.
Sure. I initially was using the DFRobot 5v Solar manager boards, but couldn't get stable results. Further research after I bought several of them indicate they have a reputation for being noisy. So, I ordered a Waveshare Solar Power Manager (D) board, hooked up to the RAK3401 via USB-C and out of the 5v green port to the 5v-EXT, with the jumper set to external power. It worked great at home. At home, it had a ALFA 915 5dbi antenna directly mounted on the bulkhead adapter on the top of the box. When I deployed it downtown, nothing changed on the inside of the box, other than hooking up the 12v solar panel to the waveshare board. Externally, I went with a Rokland 5.8dbi antenna (which was on my 4631 test node downtown), connected via a 3' piece of premade RFC-400 low loss with appropriate connectors which I also got from Rokland. I'm waiting on a second Waveshare board to be delivered to my house so I can put it on the other 1W that's there so I can eliminate that problem from the analysis loop. (edited)
andyshinn 2026-02-18 1:01 p.m.
the dfrobot board is also only 1A output max, correct? it's possible that it spikes higher. but i also wonder if in that case it's dropping voltage a lot and the PA isn't getting what it needs. on the voltaic MPPT board i had it was rated for 1A but it was dropping enough that the MCU eventually didn't recover from a brownout the waveshare is 3A. though, i haven't any real way to verify their claims... it would be ncie to get some real measurements from some of these solar boards and the RAK 1W at it's most power hungry spikes
1:03 p.m.
i keep getting closer to wanting that nordic power profiler
ThatGuy 2026-02-18 1:13 p.m.
I think the DFRobot is actually 900ma.
andyshinn 2026-02-18 1:16 p.m.
i forgot to save my tests... i had the 5V going through the INA3221 at one point and could "spam" the telemetry refresh button on the repeater to watch the current jump up briefly. not sure how accurate that was. i think i was at 19dBm set and getting up to 500mA. might be worth lowering the power slightly and see if it performans any differently
ThatGuy 2026-02-18 1:18 p.m.
I want to say I set it up at like 28 or 29 on the power. If i set it back to something lower, aren't I just defeating the purpose of having the higher power board, if the 4631 maxes out at 22? If I set the 3401 to 22, doesn't that make it essentially a more expensive and cludgy 4631?
andyshinn 2026-02-18 1:22 p.m.
well it's still much higher than the 4631. at 19dBm the 1W is going to be somewhere around 500 mW
ThatGuy
I want to say I set it up at like 28 or 29 on the power. If i set it back to something lower, aren't I just defeating the purpose of having the higher power board, if the 4631 maxes out at 22? If I set the 3401 to 22, doesn't that make it essentially a more expensive and cludgy 4631?
deej 2026-02-18 1:22 p.m.
I've read somewhere that the chip itself is only capable of 22. It gets the higher power because of an amplifier (LNA). So setting it to 22 should give you the maximum power that it can transmit, which should be higher than the 4631 set at the same 22.
1
1
andyshinn 2026-02-18 1:23 p.m.
trying to see if they published a translation table somewhere...
1:24 p.m.
ah yea they did, here we go: https://docs.rakwireless.com/product-categories/wisblock/rak13302/datasheet/#transmit-power-vs-peak-current
RAK13302 WisBlock LPWAN Wireless Module Datasheet
Provides comprehensive information about your RAK13302 to help you use it. This information includes technical specifications, characteristics, and requirements, and it also discusses the device components.
1
1:24 p.m.
so 19 ends up closer to 27 which would be around 500mW
ThatGuy 2026-02-18 1:25 p.m.
Very interesting.
andyshinn 2026-02-18 1:25 p.m.
so not full power but a bump. but also a drop in consumption at the 5V port
ThatGuy 2026-02-18 1:27 p.m.
So, theoretically, if we went with something like 20, it wouldn't be running it balls to the wall, which I never like to do in an amp circuit, but would still be getting us up in the high 20s on db
andyshinn 2026-02-18 1:28 p.m.
another silly thing i noticed... is that the whole thing does work even if you remove power from the 5V but power via USB and have no jumper set at all (effectively disabling the PA). this was working fine for me on the bench with radios close by (super terrible output) but is misleading when comes time to actually traansmit distances
1:29 p.m.
though, that doesn't really sound like what you were experiencing
ThatGuy 2026-02-18 1:29 p.m.
Also worth noting, I'm seeing a noise floor from around -100 to -92 or so.
1:30 p.m.
It bounces around on each refresh of repeater stats
andyshinn 2026-02-18 1:31 p.m.
that sounds similar to what i am seeing. noisier for sure so it's going to have a harder time getting those weaker packets
1:32 p.m.
it's such a new radio i think the jury is still out. were gathering the data so we can all compare in a couple weeks to say "yea this radio is good" or "na it kind of sucks"
ThatGuy 2026-02-18 1:32 p.m.
Right now, I'm having a hard time seeing the point, honestly.
1:33 p.m.
If it's less receptive, it doesn't really matter what the transmit power is.
1:33 p.m.
A receiver that can't hear can't repeat.
andyshinn 2026-02-18 1:35 p.m.
i guess i only have anecdotal info. but from my test locations, some where i have zero packets making it through to a repeater on my roof, i got some through to it... while the thing is sitting sideways on my office floor
andyshinn 2026-02-18 1:43 p.m.
welp i was trying to compare noise floor with the ikoka E22 radio but that one is just pegged at -120 for me and doesn't change not sure if it is even working

Is anyone getting stable production-ready results on the 3401? I had one working (or so I thought) at my house up in a tree. Took the box, mounted it on a frame, and took it to the site. I'm getting substantially worse performance from it than I was the 4631 test node I had at the site before. I basically swapped the two nodes. Eddie 2026-02-18 6:10 p.m.
I just received my kit and so far its been disappointing! I have it powered by USB with the same antenna as my other devices (other devices have batteries from their OEM). Bluetooth seems to drop just a few feet or even inches away. This happens on both of my Android phones (Pixel 9 Pro daily driver) and I plan on resurrecting a third phone that is in the box for ewaste recycling since that would be an older BLE standard. I can leave my other devices upstairs in the window, go downstairs below them and no BLE issues. Based on the absence of a message saying BLE has connected, it seems that it only sees a FRACTION of the packets that the ThinkNode M1 sees. But there's more... those missing packets in the RX log are not just from BLE dropping...the LORA side of the house is only seeing fraction of the packets since I see only a fraction of the messages in the public channel. My initial testing was on 1.13 and I upgraded my other devices from 1.12 to 1.13 to try to make it as scientific as possible. I downgraded to 1.12 (full erase) and the results are the same. I don't have any temp sensors, but its been cool to the touch so far. I am considering trying MeshTastic to see if it has the issues I have seen to see if it is bad hardware, but my experience with MT was so abysmal I hate that do that. 8-)
Skye 2026-02-18 6:13 p.m.
Yikes
Skye
The product page is confusing it makes it sound like it requires usb AND 5V AND battery, is that not the case
Eddie 2026-02-18 6:23 p.m.
This explains the person I saw on Youtube who did a solar build and seemed confused. I took it to mean either option and their FAQ is worded better "...module must draw power either from USB, an external 5V adapter, or the WisBlock Base Board’s regulated battery supply...". I am using a beefy USB battery that I hope is supplying the correct voltage. I just ordered a highly rated USB power meter so I can verify what voltage since based on RAK's web site and info here this could be the cause of issues. (edited)
Eddie
This explains the person I saw on Youtube who did a solar build and seemed confused. I took it to mean either option and their FAQ is worded better "...module must draw power either from USB, an external 5V adapter, or the WisBlock Base Board’s regulated battery supply...". according to https://docs.rakwireless.com/product-categories/meshtastic/wismesh-starter-kit-rak3401/quickstart/#power-selector-jumper it still says:
With jumper set to external 5 VDC supply with 1.5 A through connector on the RAK13302. This will supply the whole setup, Base Board, Core module and 1W transceiver. It will as well charge a battery if connected.

ThatGuy 2026-02-19 5:55 p.m.

5:56 p.m.

andyshinn 2026-02-19 5:58 p.m.
not sure if just a bug with agc or it is just how the noise floor looks when constantly resetting agc but i am noting that the noise floor maxes out at -120 when setting the agc interval to something like 4. not sure if anyone has played with the agc reset much yet
ThatGuy 2026-02-19 6:04 p.m.
I saw someone reference that, but I'm not familiar with what that is/does. Can you clarify?
andyshinn 2026-02-19 6:07 p.m.
i don't really know how it interacts with the noise floor and actual performance. i just know that it was a topic for previous 1W radios like the B&Q Station G2 where the automatic gain on the receive side could continue to creep up to a point where it eventually was almost completely deaf and there was benefit to setting the AGC reset to an interval that reset it periodically to keep it in check, which helped realy world performance

6:08 p.m.
can be set via remote command on repeaters

TF-Mambo 2026-02-20 8:41 a.m.
Got a response from RAK. Seems the Buzzer module has a LOW and HIGH setting that needs to be set low. Considering we are still developing this FW, I removed it, which I thought I had done before, and temp has stabilized with custom FW. I will keep looking around the FW to see what else can be fixed and corrected, ie. the constant Blue LED.
1
TF-Mambo 2026-02-20 8:51 a.m.
Current max temp is 97*F without the Buzzer installed. I will keep testing
TF-Mambo 2026-02-20 8:59 a.m.
Looks like stabilized at 106F/ 41C. (edited)
andyshinn 2026-02-20 10:59 a.m.
that still seems awfully warm? i guess i can check out mine before i place it on the roof and see what the temp is
ThatGuy 2026-02-20 11:16 a.m.
That is really odd. When I went to bed last night, the temps on my two were at 58F and 63F I think. (edited)
11:21 a.m.
Current Ambient: 56F Node Temps: 58F and 66*F
11:26 a.m.
Back to the receive issue for a moment: As reported, I drove down and applied the agc.reset.interval CLI command to my problem-child repeater. Immediate signal improvement. On the test repeater at my house, I had applied a custom FW that had RX BOOST turned off. I also applied the AGC fix to it. Things were working well. To test, I flashed it back to standard v1.12 firmware. Performance dropped. I'm going to put it back to the custom firmware sometime this afternoon hopefully, to see if it returns to better signal. I think we may be making some progress on this issue.
ThatGuy
Back to the receive issue for a moment: As reported, I drove down and applied the agc.reset.interval CLI command to my problem-child repeater. Immediate signal improvement. On the test repeater at my house, I had applied a custom FW that had RX BOOST turned off. I also applied the AGC fix to it. Things were working well. To test, I flashed it back to standard v1.12 firmware. Performance dropped. I'm going to put it back to the custom firmware sometime this afternoon hopefully, to see if it returns to better signal. I think we may be making some progress on this issue.
TF-Mambo 2026-02-20 11:39 a.m.
That is great. Yes, we will continue to look into the code and fix things that may be wonky. I sent a reply email to RAK about the stable temp to see what they say.
TF-Mambo
That is great. Yes, we will continue to look into the code and fix things that may be wonky. I sent a reply email to RAK about the stable temp to see what they say.

andyshinn 2026-02-20 3:37 p.m.
well my battery died pretty early in the voltage drop. the waveshare seems to cut off 5V at around <3.4V so that was much earlier than expected
andyshinn 2026-02-20 3:52 p.m.

TF-Mambo 2026-02-20 5:14 p.m.
Running into an interesting issue compiling custom for BLE. If I add -D RAK_BOARD to the [rak3401] build_flags and -D PIN_GPS_EN=-1 to the [env:RAK_3401_companion_radio_ble] build_flags, I get GPS enabled and working but has slow channel and contact loads and does not communicate with LoRa. Without these two build_flags I get no GPS but works fine. Thoughts?
andyshinn 2026-02-20 5:19 p.m.
i believe -1 makes it go through a GPS auto-detection part for RAK. i would maybe first check that https://docs.rakwireless.com/product-categories/wisblock/rak13302/datasheet/#pin-definition doesn't step on any pins used by the GPS sensor. different sensor ports sometimes have different IO pin usages. also, the 19007 has different IO pins given to the sensor depending on the port so maybe confirming with https://docs.rakwireless.com/product-categories/wisblock/rak19007/datasheet/#connectors-for-wisblock-sensor if any of the IO pins are shared bby that sensor
RAK19007 WisBlock Base Board 2nd Gen Datasheet | Specs & Features
View specs for the RAK19007 WisBlock Base Board 2nd Gen, including power, voltage input, temperature range, and compatibility with WisBlock modules.
RAK13302 WisBlock LPWAN Wireless Module Datasheet
Provides comprehensive information about your RAK13302 to help you use it. This information includes technical specifications, characteristics, and requirements, and it also discusses the device components.
TF-Mambo 2026-02-20 5:22 p.m.
Thanks for the info. Let me see what the docs say.
andyshinn 2026-02-20 5:27 p.m.
what GPS sensor is it?
TF-Mambo 2026-02-20 5:39 p.m.
12500
5:40 p.m.
I looked at all the docs including the 12500 doc and don't see anything. what i did notice is the 12500 doc says to use slot A or C and the 19007 doc says you can use Slot D for GNSS.
andyshinn 2026-02-20 5:44 p.m.
believe there was some slot that had a GPS conflict on MT but now i forget what it was... it needed to be in a specific slot
TF-Mambo 2026-02-20 5:45 p.m.
I will try to put in Slot A and see but I thought I had already tried that.
andyshinn 2026-02-20 5:49 p.m.
yea there is a conflict on slot D and the user button on with the GPS. it needs to be in slot A on the 19007
TF-Mambo 2026-02-20 5:56 p.m.
OK. played musical Slots with the sensors and no GPS with default FW. Slot A - 12500, Slot B - Empty, Slot C- 1906, Slot D - 12002. (edited)
5:57 p.m.
The 1906 works fine and RTC I think just works, no way to test, to my knowledge.
6:02 p.m.
let me see if I enable those two build_flags now if I still get the issues.
6:08 p.m.
PROGRESS!!!! So now with the build_flags enabled it loads fine and LoRa works. I will test this further. IF this works, now I have to figure out why the blue light stays on instead of just a mild blink when connected.

possibly because i've got 1W on both sides (my companion is a Ikoka Handheld at the moment) but i had some really good results doing to tests today. i had good repeats in places that i was not able to get anything before. i'll need to do more testing with a regular 4631 handheld to see if it exhibits any deafness when the other side is lower powered. but so far it didn't seem like it was very deaf at all to me

I've got the nightly build and it seems to be behaving from a noise floor perspective and does not feel warm. Is anyone able to assist with a firmware build of nightly (or whatever is the best working right now) with the observer flags turned on? -D MESH_PACKET_LOGGING=1 flag enabled using PLATFORMIO_BUILD_FLAGS. ThatGuy 2026-02-24 1:15 p.m.
So, I tried building a version of the 1.13 FW that had the toggle for RXBoost from that PR I shared recently. It worked on my RAK3401 at home, but when I installed it on my problem child repeater, it became unresponsive. After a couple of reinstalls, I fell back to one that just has RXBooster compiled off. It installed and the repeater came back up. Not sure what the issue was. Since install, we've noticed a significant increase in performance since turning RX Boost off. I think if you have a noisy environment, this is going to give significant improvements when using this RAK 3401/13302 board. I can't explain why it's working by citing specific stats like RSSI values etc, but it's connecting to things it wasn't before, and is passing traffic more reliably. (edited)
2
ThatGuy
So, I tried building a version of the 1.13 FW that had the toggle for RXBoost from that PR I shared recently. It worked on my RAK3401 at home, but when I installed it on my problem child repeater, it became unresponsive. After a couple of reinstalls, I fell back to one that just has RXBooster compiled off. It installed and the repeater came back up. Not sure what the issue was. Since install, we've noticed a significant increase in performance since turning RX Boost off. I think if you have a noisy environment, this is going to give significant improvements when using this RAK 3401/13302 board. I can't explain why it's working by citing specific stats like RSSI values etc, but it's connecting to things it wasn't before, and is passing traffic more reliably. (edited)

ThatGuy 2026-02-24 4:01 p.m.

4:02 p.m.
4:03 p.m.
"Range & RF Performance: SX1262 + SKY66122 Amplified Path The WisMesh 1W Booster Starter Kit delivers exceptional performance for users needing a long-range Meshtastic solution. This is done by combining the Semtech SX1262 LoRa transceiver with the Skyworks SKY66122 high-power amplifier and a dedicated SAW filter. Unlike typical high-power boards that boost TX but degrade RX, this filtered RF chain, including an RF filter and PA for improved receiver sensitivity, preserves receiver performance while increasing output power up to 1 watt. The result is a cleaner signal, reduced self-interference, and significantly stronger SNR across real-world deployments. Users can expect more reliable two-way links, improved packet retention at long distances, and stable mesh coverage even in semi-obstructed environments."
4:03 p.m.
weebl2000 2026-02-24 4:05 p.m.
yeah so it has an LNA
4:06 p.m.
I have the Heltec v4 and the setup is similar, there's PA/LNA (just a different chip, GC1109)
4:06 p.m.
the SX1262 can suffer from "deafness" even without the PA/LNA - it just makes it worse/more likely
ThatGuy 2026-02-24 4:07 p.m.
Yeah, I'm getting much better performance out of this board by turning on agc.reset.interval and turning off the RX Boost.
weebl2000 2026-02-24 4:07 p.m.
with strong signals/interference it's likely to get stuck in a low gain mode

the current firmware doesn't actually reset the "agc" properly (the SX1262 doesn't really have a true AGC)
4:08 p.m.
i dived into the datasheet and implemented AGC reset for SX1262, it properly resets the gain on the chip

Took it downtown to a rooftop node, and it would flash, but then the board was inaccessible. Tried reflashing several times. Ended up going back to a FW that I had that just hard-coded the RX Boost feature off at compile time, and it came right back up. (edited)

ThatGuy 2026-02-24 4:56 p.m.

weebl2000 2026-02-24 4:56 p.m.
weird
ThatGuy 2026-02-24 4:56 p.m.

4:57 p.m.

weebl2000 2026-02-24 4:58 p.m.
seems like an unlikely culprit
4:59 p.m.

ThatGuy 2026-02-24 5:01 p.m.
It's just odd that it behaved differently on two pieces of identical hardware.
5:03 p.m.
The one downtown may not have the OTAFIX bootloader. I can't remember. But, I was doing it via USB cable using the flasher.
weebl2000 2026-02-24 5:03 p.m.
i think the PR you linked is inherently broken
ThatGuy 2026-02-24 5:20 p.m.
In what way?
weebl2000 2026-02-24 5:23 p.m.
they read the register for boosted gain, to read getRxBoostedGainMode as a boolean
5:24 p.m.
but there's two values 0x94 (power saving) and 0x96 (boosted gain)
5:24 p.m.
it will always return true
ThatGuy 2026-02-24 5:26 p.m.
Interesting.
ThatGuy 2026-02-24 5:43 p.m.
It looks like up in src/helpers/CommonCLI.cpp, they constrain that preference to either a 0 or a 1 if I'm reading it correctly.
weebl2000 2026-02-24 5:44 p.m.
yeah, but not sure what happens if you store 0
ThatGuy 2026-02-24 5:44 p.m.
me either.
5:45 p.m.
Actually, I do. Hang on.
weebl2000 2026-02-24 5:45 p.m.
try setting it to 0, then reboot and fetch the preference setting again
5:48 p.m.
i think the side effect might be that it worked but the reading of the status is definitely not correct
ThatGuy 2026-02-24 5:49 p.m.
I already had it off.
5:50 p.m.
weebl2000 2026-02-24 5:51 p.m.
https://github.com/jbrazio/MeshCore/pull/1/changes
Fix remote LNA toggle bugs: correct register comparison, add missin...
getRxBoostedGain always returned true because both 0x94 (power saving) and 0x96 (boosted gain = 1) return true
5:52 p.m.
yeah the PR probably worked, but logging / debugging would give inconsistent log messages
ThatGuy 2026-02-24 5:52 p.m.
Cool. Nice catch

weebl2000 2026-02-24 6:33 p.m.
http://mcimages.weebl.me/?commitId=rak3401-agc-and-lna-remote if you're up for it - it contains the agc reset fix, the lna remote toggling and this PR i've just opened https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/pull/1836/
Fix RAK3401 SKY66122-11 FEM control: enable CSD/CPS for proper PA/L...
The RAK13302 1W module uses a Skyworks SKY66122-11 front-end module with three digital control pins (CSD, CTX, CPS) that must be actively driven by the host MCU. The previous code only managed CTX ...
ThatGuy 2026-02-24 6:35 p.m.
Do you think this improper control could have somehow explained the weird power/overheating issues some people were seeing with this board? (edited)
ThatGuy
Do you think this improper control could have somehow explained the weird power/overheating issues some people were seeing with this board? (edited)
weebl2000 2026-02-24 6:42 p.m.
very plausible, a lot of pins were left floating in states causing unpredictable behiavor
ThatGuy 2026-02-24 6:45 p.m.
Very, very interesting.
weebl2000 2026-02-24 6:55 p.m.
if you notice any improvement, please comment on the PR because i have no way of verifying anything of this actually works in practice (edited)
6:55 p.m.

Fix RAK3401 SKY66122-11 FEM control: enable CSD/CPS for proper PA/L...
The RAK13302 1W module uses a Skyworks SKY66122-11 front-end module with three digital control pins (CSD, CTX, CPS) that must be actively driven by the host MCU. The previous code only managed CTX ...

ThatGuy 2026-02-24 10:58 p.m.
Installed the v4 version. With ‘set radio.lna off’ I’m seeing a consistent 3-3.5 db increase in advert receive. I’ve turned it on and off multiple times.
ccw 2026-02-24 11:27 p.m.
I’ve got one of the boosted raks and have been a little mystified by its behavior, so I’ve been trying to follow the conversation a bit. I don’t want to interrupt, but if you’ve got a second, could you expand on that a bit? Or, rather, am I understanding correctly that you’re just saying when you turn the amplifier off, the apparent sensitivity improves?
ccw
I’ve got one of the boosted raks and have been a little mystified by its behavior, so I’ve been trying to follow the conversation a bit. I don’t want to interrupt, but if you’ve got a second, could you expand on that a bit? Or, rather, am I understanding correctly that you’re just saying when you turn the amplifier off, the apparent sensitivity improves?
ThatGuy 2026-02-25 11:27 a.m.
@ccw I'm not an expert, and there are several simultaneous issues going on here, so it can be kind of confusing. I'll explain my understanding of it, but there's a fair chance I don't fully understand. For a while, people have been observing that the RAK 1W boards, particularly in noisy RF environments, are "going deaf". Speculation on this has focus around a couple of different areas. 1) the automatic gain controller essentially dialing up the gain until the receiver overloads and can't hear anything through the noise, and then not properly resetting when the noise floor decreases. The AGC is supposed to adjust itself automatically. In order to force the board to reset it, people have been trying 'set agc.reset.interval ' to make the board reset itself every _ seconds (It has to be a multiple of 4). Some people have seen performance increases by doing so. I am one of them. It wasn't huge, but it was noticable. 2) There is also speculation that since the 13302 board has its own reception booster and filter on board, that the built in software RX Boost, which is turned on in the MC firmware by default it essentially causing a "double boost", and making the receiver go deaf. Some people have also seen performance increases by disabling the software RX Boost. Initially, you had to do this by compiling an custom firmware to turn it off, b/c the setting was housed in a compile time build flag. Somone created a PR that makes it user selectable in the repeater command line interface via the ' {get/set} radio.lna {on/off}' command. Then, yesterday, @weebl2000 discovered that the FW may not have had all of the necessary configuration for the 13302 board to be correctly powered and used, which may have been contributing to some of the issues. He patched the code to fix that. Some people also experience overheating issues with the 1W boards. Could be software problem. Might be defective hardware. (edited)
2
ccw 2026-02-25 12:58 p.m.
Thanks for the thorough summary! Doubling up on hardware and software signal boosting does sound unhelpful. Looking forward to trying some of the patched firmware this evening
1
ThatGuy 2026-02-25 1:07 p.m.
I guess my next question to @weebl2000 then is this: Does issuing the command "set radio.lna off' disable only the software boost, or is it turning off the 13302's hardware boost as well? (edited)
1:08 p.m.
Also, @weebl2000, are these firmwares being built on the main branch or the dev branch? Someone asked me and I didn't know the answer.
ThatGuy
@ccw I'm not an expert, and there are several simultaneous issues going on here, so it can be kind of confusing. I'll explain my understanding of it, but there's a fair chance I don't fully understand. For a while, people have been observing that the RAK 1W boards, particularly in noisy RF environments, are "going deaf". Speculation on this has focus around a couple of different areas. 1) the automatic gain controller essentially dialing up the gain until the receiver overloads and can't hear anything through the noise, and then not properly resetting when the noise floor decreases. The AGC is supposed to adjust itself automatically. In order to force the board to reset it, people have been trying 'set agc.reset.interval ' to make the board reset itself every _ seconds (It has to be a multiple of 4). Some people have seen performance increases by doing so. I am one of them. It wasn't huge, but it was noticable. 2) There is also speculation that since the 13302 board has its own reception booster and filter on board, that the built in software RX Boost, which is turned on in the MC firmware by default it essentially causing a "double boost", and making the receiver go deaf. Some people have also seen performance increases by disabling the software RX Boost. Initially, you had to do this by compiling an custom firmware to turn it off, b/c the setting was housed in a compile time build flag. Somone created a PR that makes it user selectable in the repeater command line interface via the ' {get/set} radio.lna {on/off}' command. Then, yesterday, @weebl2000 discovered that the FW may not have had all of the necessary configuration for the 13302 board to be correctly powered and used, which may have been contributing to some of the issues. He patched the code to fix that. Some people also experience overheating issues with the 1W boards. Could be software problem. Might be defective hardware. (edited)

Aenigma 2026-02-25 8:38 p.m.
Hello all - new member here and new Meshcore user. I have one of these 1W RAK kits on order so I’m following along closely. I wanted to commend you for your determination to optimize this firmware. One quick question: what SX1262 power out level setting are you using? Reading the SKY66122-11 datasheet, a max input power level is +16dBm. I know the SX1262 can go up to +22dBm so I was curious how that aspect was being handled. The typical insertion loss of the SAW filter is about 2dB so I would think any SX1262 output power setting over 18dB might start over-driving the PA. Also, on the RX side for the SX1262, I don’t think the boosted gain setting is necessary seeing that the SKY66122-11 has 16dB of gain. Boosting in the SX1262 will just amplify the noise seeing the SKY66122-11 has just an OK noise figure. Anyway, not sure if this is helpful but wanted to say thanks again for what you are doing! (edited)
1
ThatGuy 2026-02-25 10:46 p.m.
Interesting! Thanks! There is a published chart of the DB-setting equivalencies. 22 in the firmware = 30 db output. Based on discussion here, I recently backed mine down to around 18 or 19 for testing purposes.
ThatGuy 2026-02-25 10:54 p.m.
10:54 p.m.
from https://docs.rakwireless.com/product-categories/wisblock/RAK13302/datasheet/
Aenigma 2026-02-25 11:41 p.m.
Thanks! Maybe RAK handles this in the 13302 module such that a setting of 22 will safely achieve the max output with the PA. I just never like pushing things to the limit and I was wondering if some of the overheating concerns were related to TX power settings, how the power is supplied to the kit (external vs internal), or something else (I saw some mentions of a buzzer circuit). (edited)

Cisien
What would i be looking for? Better snr? Lower noise floor?
weebl2000 2026-02-26 8:15 p.m.
better SNR mostly
8:16 p.m.
@Cisien it's a difficult thing to test, enabling/disabling the LNA. But if you have one device the easiest test is: see if you see higher receive RSSI on your RAK 3401 for any other device that is sending with the same power at the same distance
8:17 p.m.
if you have two devices, then you would hope to see: 1. more packets being received at the edge of your RX capability - 2. higher RSSI and ideally SNR for the received packets
8:17 p.m.
if you know your way around electronics and have have good probes for a multimeter then you could measure voltage across pins on the SKY66122-11 FEM when you're in receive mode
8:18 p.m.
but if you've never probed an IC chip and don't feel comfortable around a multimeter that last one is probably not the best step, it can be pretty dangerous if you short out the wrong pins on the chip
8:18 p.m.
and you definitely need a magnifier glass to see the pins because they're soo tiny
8:20 p.m.
8:20 p.m.
for my heltec V4 and GC1109 it will look like this without zooming in
8:20 p.m.
and the pins you'd need to measure are these 4 tiny blobs
8:21 p.m.
this chip:
8:22 p.m.
8:22 p.m.
Image for scale my thumb is the width of the board

ThatGuy 2026-02-26 10:28 p.m.
I tested mine as follows: Installed the v4 variant on my 3401 , rebooted. Logged into a remote repeater and sent an advert. Logged back into the modded 3401 repeater, and looked at the neighbors list with the radio.lna on. Recorded the observed SNR. Set radio.lna off, rebooted, and repeated the process. Results: Lna on: 5.75 db, 3.5 Lna off: 8.0 db, 8.5, 9.5. (edited)
ThatGuy
I tested mine as follows: Installed the v4 variant on my 3401 , rebooted. Logged into a remote repeater and sent an advert. Logged back into the modded 3401 repeater, and looked at the neighbors list with the radio.lna on. Recorded the observed SNR. Set radio.lna off, rebooted, and repeated the process. Results: Lna on: 5.75 db, 3.5 Lna off: 8.0 db, 8.5, 9.5. (edited)

10:40 p.m.
with lora you can make out signals with very negative SNR ratios
10:41 p.m.
so the only real test is having two devices side-by-side and seeing succesful decode rates (edited)
Aenigma
So LNA On/Off refers to the “boost” mode being on/off in the SX1262 and doesn’t change anything in the front-end module (SKY66122, which has an LNA), correct?
ccw 2026-02-26 10:41 p.m.

@ThatGuy I'd love to see you try the dev_plus firmware with agc.reset.interval set to like 8
Aenigma 2026-02-26 10:42 p.m.
yeah but you are amplifying the noise coming from the FEM too. It doesn’t have the best noise figure (2.2dB I believe) so the boost stage in the SX1262 would amplify the noise and the signal
ThatGuy 2026-02-26 10:42 p.m.
I have agc.reset.interval set to 16 on both boxes (edited)
Aenigma
yeah but you are amplifying the noise coming from the FEM too. It doesn’t have the best noise figure (2.2dB I believe) so the boost stage in the SX1262 would amplify the noise and the signal
ccw 2026-02-26 10:42 p.m.
For sure, it seems like you want one or the other at most
ThatGuy 2026-02-26 10:43 p.m.
And the distant repeater has RXBoost compiled off
ccw
For sure, it seems like you want one or the other at most
weebl2000 2026-02-26 10:43 p.m.
it's really hard to compare if you can't compare two devices side-by-side with one having it one and the other off
10:43 p.m.
you can't compare SNR with just 1 device, it doesn't tell you how much it does/doesn't pick up on otherwise
Aenigma 2026-02-26 10:43 p.m.

3 days ago my PR that implements proper AGC reset for SX1262 for meshtastic was merged into develop tho https://github.com/meshtastic/firmware/pull/9705
Implement 'agc' reset for SX126x & LR11x0 chip families by weebl200...
There&amp;#39;s no actual agc on SX126x chips but you can reset the analog registers by doing a warm sleep &amp;amp; running calibration. I saw there were earlier attempts #8163 but reverted in #97...

so you can try it out on meshtastic develop

Aenigma 2026-02-26 10:59 p.m.
So this reset logic, that’s really just doing what happens on a POR automatically, right? But on a set interval? I’m looking at section 9.2 of the datasheet. The AGC calibration registers, which maps RSSI values, is in section 6.1.6 but would require specific power levels to be applied from a signal generator. I assume (maybe wrongly?) that Semtec pre-calibrates the devices? (edited)

Aenigma
So this reset logic, that’s really just doing what happens on a POR automatically, right? But on a set interval? I’m looking at section 9.2 of the datasheet. The AGC calibration registers, which maps RSSI values, is in section 6.1.6 but would require specific power levels to be applied from a signal generator. I assume (maybe wrongly?) that Semtec pre-calibrates the devices? (edited)
weebl2000 2026-02-26 11:07 p.m.
What agc reset does basically is reset the analog registers on the chip. Essentially it starts learning gain from scratch again
11:08 p.m.
There's no true agc on the SX1262
11:08 p.m.
And with strong signals it's prone to get stuck on a low gain that makes it somewhat deaf
Aenigma 2026-02-26 11:16 p.m.
Ok so no code affects the AGC registers noted in section 6.1.6, correct? I am curious how those get set initially since it is dependent on external matching, filtering, etc.
weebl2000
try now

Aenigma 2026-02-27 1:08 a.m.
I guess I have to question https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/pull/1836 CSD and CPS on the SKY66122 are both connected to WisBlock IO3 per https://docs.rakwireless.com/product-categories/wisblock/RAK13302/datasheet/ Setting IO3 to logic high would take it from shutdown/sleep to enabling the LNA on the SKY66122. Also, CTX is connected directly to the DIO2 pin on the SX1262, assuming that the SX1262 has been setup with “SetDIO2AsRfSwitchCtrl” in section 11.3 of the SX1262 datasheet. Similarly, the circuit is setup such that the SX1262 controls the TCXO via its DIO3 which would require “SetDIO3AsTcxoCtrl”. Finally, the boost regulator, U5, is controlled by WisBlock IO2 and only necessary if a battery is being used (jumper set to IN_5V) versus directly powering the RAK13302 via the 5V connection on the bottom and setting the jumper to EX_5V. My thoughts/theory is that maybe the control I/Os aren’t being properly handled per the circuit intention/implementation. If the CSD/CPS (IO3) is going low after TX for whatever reason, then the SKY66122 is effectively being turned off and that may explain the unit going “deaf”. (edited)
Fix RAK3401 SKY66122-11 FEM control: enable CSD/CPS for proper PA/L...
preamble: I was talking to someone about their RAK3401 board on Discord and dived into datasheets and current implementation. I asked them to test and come back with findings, but I strongly believ...
Aenigma 2026-02-27 1:18 a.m.
Hopefully that all makes sense. I read the comments in the PR regarding the control pins and it didn’t match what was shown on the schematics.
Aenigma 2026-02-27 1:37 a.m.
All of my comments above rely on https://docs.rakwireless.com/product-categories/wisblock/RAK13302/datasheet/ being accurate but I am seeing discrepancies between the schematics and the connector diagram. For example, EX_5V (via Schottky D1) should not be connected to USB+ (Pin 7) on the board-to-board connector and have it power the RAK3401 properly - it should be connected to VBUS (Pin 9). The whole documentation is a bit of a mess from RAK…
Fokai 2026-02-27 1:40 a.m.
@weebl2000 This is good. Initial 1hr of goofing around in my opinion it's better on receive than the v4 LNA and LNA bypass on their newest dev version. TX is very good. I'll know more in the morning after observer collects all the data and I can pick through it a bit. (edited)
Aenigma 2026-02-27 1:47 a.m.
One last thought then I’m done for the day: how are folks powering this kit? Could the boost converter (if you are using the IN_5V path with a battery connected, charging via USB) be causing extra noise to be present in the circuit, raising the noise floor? Has anyone done an A-B test between IN_5V and EX_5V (no battery connected)? Huh I just looked closer and EN on the boost converter (U5) is NC to the IO2 connection on the WisBlock so it would always be enabled if a battery is connected.
Fokai 2026-02-27 1:53 a.m.
I've got a funky power setup. USB-C PD 20v to repeater box at mast, down convert to 5v into a PI, then USB out of PI to RAK with a battery connected. I could try off the 5v rails from the PI one of these days...
Aenigma
I guess I have to question https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/pull/1836 CSD and CPS on the SKY66122 are both connected to WisBlock IO3 per https://docs.rakwireless.com/product-categories/wisblock/RAK13302/datasheet/ Setting IO3 to logic high would take it from shutdown/sleep to enabling the LNA on the SKY66122. Also, CTX is connected directly to the DIO2 pin on the SX1262, assuming that the SX1262 has been setup with “SetDIO2AsRfSwitchCtrl” in section 11.3 of the SX1262 datasheet. Similarly, the circuit is setup such that the SX1262 controls the TCXO via its DIO3 which would require “SetDIO3AsTcxoCtrl”. Finally, the boost regulator, U5, is controlled by WisBlock IO2 and only necessary if a battery is being used (jumper set to IN_5V) versus directly powering the RAK13302 via the 5V connection on the bottom and setting the jumper to EX_5V. My thoughts/theory is that maybe the control I/Os aren’t being properly handled per the circuit intention/implementation. If the CSD/CPS (IO3) is going low after TX for whatever reason, then the SKY66122 is effectively being turned off and that may explain the unit going “deaf”. (edited)
weebl2000 2026-02-27 5:33 a.m.
I think you're right. I've changed it. I suspect the previous code still worked though
Aenigma 2026-02-27 8:57 a.m.
Sounds good. I wasn’t questioning if the previous code worked or not just that it didn’t line up with the hardware design. Hopefully we can trust RAK’s schematics.
Aenigma 2026-02-27 9:08 a.m.
Are folks still having overheating issues?
Aenigma
Are folks still having overheating issues?
ThatGuy 2026-02-27 11:44 a.m.
I have at least one guy in my group who is. I'm trying to get him to install one of these firmwares to see if it solves his issue. Waiting on a report back.
ThatGuy
I have at least one guy in my group who is. I'm trying to get him to install one of these firmwares to see if it solves his issue. Waiting on a report back.
Aenigma 2026-02-27 11:51 a.m.
I am concerned that, if the EX_5V is wired up to USB+ like it shows on the schematics, it could cause some issues with the RAK3401 module and may be a cause of the overheating. The device could be getting back-powered through ESD diodes or some other path that’s not intentional (should be VBUS). If overheating happens when using EX_5V but not when using a battery+USB input (IN_5V jumper setting), that could explain things. I have a support question out to RAK questioning their schematics and if it is indeed how the RAK13302 module is wired. (edited)
Aenigma
I am concerned that, if the EX_5V is wired up to USB+ like it shows on the schematics, it could cause some issues with the RAK3401 module and may be a cause of the overheating. The device could be getting back-powered through ESD diodes or some other path that’s not intentional (should be VBUS). If overheating happens when using EX_5V but not when using a battery+USB input (IN_5V jumper setting), that could explain things. I have a support question out to RAK questioning their schematics and if it is indeed how the RAK13302 module is wired. (edited)

I need to go down there and lay eyes on it personally. Am I understanding the revised documentation from RAK correctly that if you use the 5v EXT, that's all the power you need? No need for a battery, or USB or anything else?
Aenigma 2026-02-27 11:57 a.m.
Yes, but I think they wired it up wrong if the schematics are accurate

interesting.

shedhero 2026-02-27 12:49 p.m.
Has anyone else has noise floor issues? A couple of us have these in our local mesh but we are pretty deaf compared to a 4631. On 1.12 I had a noise floor of -120 for a day before it dropped to -102. On 1.13 it was 2 days before the noise floor dropped. But once it goes to -102 it doesn't matter if I reboot or what not, it doesn't go back up.

ccw 2026-02-27 5:28 p.m.
In case it’s a useful data point: if I power via the external 5V, and then just refresh the telemetry rapidly while not transmitting, the device reports 4-4.2V. Maybe 4.2 most of the time with drop outs. In any case, not a stable value. As a sanity check, doesn’t seem to alter the behavior if I turn the transmitter power all the way down
andyshinn 2026-02-27 5:34 p.m.
if you mean the battery voltage then that is from the charger trying to charge the battery. it isn't from the 5V rail
ccw 2026-02-27 5:46 p.m.

Fokai 2026-03-02 12:55 p.m.
Duplicate of what I posted on GitHub below. I'm liking what I'm seeing. This is #1836 - Variant 4 AGC reset set at 16. Updated Astor Repeater and tested rak3401-agc-and-lna-remote-registerpatch. It's hearing very well vs a comparable observer in capturing all packets, indicating that it is not going deaf for extended seconds at a time. I've got a hill node LOS that I put up on 1s delay in case I miss a packet while tx at this observer. Noise floor has been stable at about -102 with noise spikes correlating with high rx utilization in the graphs. I'll keep this up for a few days at least and update if any issues arise. (edited)
ThatGuy 2026-03-02 1:03 p.m.
@weebl2000 , can you give us a good recap of what's included where, and what we should be testing at this point?

ThatGuy 2026-03-02 6:28 p.m.
Out of curiosity, does your duty cycle one take into account varying regulatory structures based on location/preset? It's my understanding that here in the US, we don't have a duty cycle limit like they do in other places.
ThatGuy
Out of curiosity, does your duty cycle one take into account varying regulatory structures based on location/preset? It's my understanding that here in the US, we don't have a duty cycle limit like they do in other places.
weebl2000 2026-03-02 7:03 p.m.
Not my PR but yes, it keeps track of those.
7:04 p.m.
It does so more accurately even, by averaging the duty cycle over the past 1 hr instead of waiting after every send
1
7:05 p.m.
7:05 p.m.
i.e. with this change your repeater/room server etc can be more bursty without worrying (edited)
ThatGuy

weebl2000 2026-03-03 7:09 a.m.
100% now

7:09 a.m.
i lobbied a bit for it to be merged
1
ThatGuy 2026-03-03 11:18 a.m.
Woo hoo!
Fokai 2026-03-03 11:18 a.m.
I was asked to take a look at LNA on vs LNA off in the CLI. Both scenarios stayed up for ~12hrs and I checked the snapshop in the picture many times and it was very consistent with the LNA on performing slightly better than LNA off. Splitting hairs with ~1% improvement in packets heard for LNA ON, but it was consistently better. Again, this performance was for my particular station (Astor) environment against a closely performing monitor a few miles away that has very good PDX coverage. Overall, the PR is working very well. Nice work! This was all done with interval reset at 0. I never reset the AGC at any point during these tests. It's actually working well IMO (edited)
ThatGuy 2026-03-03 11:18 a.m.
So, what all ended up in the final package?
ThatGuy 2026-03-03 12:13 p.m.
Also, @weebl2000 , thank you for your very hard work on the code on this, and to everyone else who helped test it. (edited)
1
Lbibass 2026-03-03 2:33 p.m.
It’s been working GREAT in my tree node
2:33 p.m.
2:34 p.m.
I’m also using a ceramic filter from ZBM for some extra filtering because the antenna I’m using right now picks up a ton of out of band stuff
paul / BostonMesh 2026-03-03 5:27 p.m.

5:29 p.m.
The ZMB filters are great - we are using them for heltec v4 deployments. The promise of the 1W Rak booster is that it already has a filter.
ccw 2026-03-03 6:03 p.m.

ThatGuy 2026-03-05 3:14 p.m.
So, in case anyone is interested, after I drove off last night, I discovered that the replacement board was not working as intended. Couldn't hit it from more than a 3-4 blocks away. Re-flashed the one I took off last night with weebl2000's latest above , and took it back down there at lunch today, reinstalled after some troubleshooting, and it's working marvelously. I'm getting direct connect reception where I've never have before. I'm thinking I've got a bad transmitter section on the new one I tried to put up last night. It was hearing OK, but it wouldn't respond and nothing could hear it outside of its immediate area. Going to have to do some testing on it. QC seems to be an issue on these 1W boards.
ccw 2026-03-05 3:16 p.m.
Curious to hear what you find. I was disappointed with mine and then it was all of a sudden amazing, getting reception where it never had and nothing else would. I chalked it up to the firmware but tbh it didn’t 100% correlate with switching
3:17 p.m.
(Seemed like it kept getting better for a while after)
ThatGuy 2026-03-05 3:18 p.m.
I guess we'll see. I like consistency, and these boards are anything but that.
ccw 2026-03-05 3:21 p.m.
Plenty of teething on hardware, firmware, and software to get through. Seems like it’ll be a while before consistency is likely
3:21 p.m.
(But agreed, it’s frustrating)

thetooth 2026-03-09 12:10 a.m.
also reporting another success with this fw. i am about to put up a repeater and did some testing of the solar charging before going on the roof, stock fw i was only getting 1.5 days from a 2400mAh battery with sun hitting the panel so figured the charging circuit was bad but checking the current it was pulling 50mA continuously which is obviously not normal. this firmware I am seeing basically nothing and still around 50% state of charge after 3 days without solar connected
3

WisMesh 1W Booster Starter Kit
The WisMesh 1W Booster Starter Kit brings more TX power and better reception to Meshtastic networks. Powered by the RAK3401 WisBlock Core module with the Nordic nRF52840 MCU and a RAK13302 1W LoRa transceiver module, mounted on a Rak19007 Base board. Unlike common solutions, the RAK13302 integrates a dedicated RF fil
9:44 p.m.
I don’t plan to use it in companion mode (gonna live in a box on a tower as a repeater) but I could see people wanting to take advantage of the lower power nRF-based hardware versus the ESP32 hardware in companion mode. (edited)

1
curtisc83
Which seem to be a lot of money
ThatGuy 2026-03-12 12:01 p.m.
Most of it is the fees the carrier charges you to do the paperwork on both ends. I saw one guy who had a $34 import fee charge from UPS...$25 of which was their form fees, and the rest was the tarriff. DHL is apparently even worse. It also depends on whether they pick the right code for the product. Apparently, these carriers are bad about just picking the first one that might work, and not the most applicable/reasonable one that works.
ccw
Nice. Has anyone encountered this business of getting a bill from the shipper for additional money like a month after the fact?

6:49 p.m.

ThatGuy 2026-03-18 6:50 p.m.
That's quick!
6:50 p.m.

curtisc83 2026-03-18 6:51 p.m.
Might of just hit the hot air balloons at just the right time
6:52 p.m.
The timing is funny
ThatGuy 2026-03-18 6:52 p.m.
Both of my orders were moving inside China for 4-5 days before I ever got a shipping notification, and then it was another 7-10 days to get it stateside and handed over to USPS.
curtisc83 2026-03-18 6:52 p.m.
With Rokland having the delay
6:52 p.m.
Like what is Rokland waiting for?
6:52 p.m.
Just order it
ThatGuy 2026-03-18 6:55 p.m.
Fines for them for importing and reselling an unlicensed product can be EXTREMELY expensive for a domestic company. Like thousands of dollars a day per occurrence, I read.
curtisc83 2026-03-18 6:56 p.m.
Oh maybe one of you know. How can I tell if these ceramic filters improve things
6:56 p.m.
Is there like a stat I can see improve or something
ThatGuy 2026-03-18 6:57 p.m.
Take a look at your noise floor and your RSSI values before and after. Try to keep everything else the same.
curtisc83 2026-03-18 6:57 p.m.
So if my noise floor is like -100 -90 is way better?
curtisc83
So if my noise floor is like -100 -90 is way better?
ThatGuy 2026-03-18 6:58 p.m.
I believe you want it going the other way.
6:58 p.m.
The lower the number the better, I think.
curtisc83 2026-03-18 6:59 p.m.
What about RSSI
6:59 p.m.
Is a -35 better than a -39
ThatGuy 2026-03-18 7:00 p.m.
On RSSI, I believe the closer to zero you are, the stronger/better the signal.
7:00 p.m.
On noise floor, the lower the number, the better, because that means its quieter.
curtisc83 2026-03-18 7:01 p.m.
So -200 is better than -100
ThatGuy 2026-03-18 7:01 p.m.
On noise floor, yes.
7:02 p.m.
On rssi, no.
curtisc83 2026-03-18 7:02 p.m.
These chips stats move so much it’s hard to gauge
ThatGuy 2026-03-18 7:04 p.m.
Yeah. You can sit there on a repeater and refresh over and over, and it will be all over the place.
7:05 p.m.
I have noticed that the 3401 boards consistently show about a 10db higher noise floor than the 4631 similarly equipped in close proximity.
curtisc83 2026-03-18 7:22 p.m.
I installed them on two 1 watt raks
7:22 p.m.
I guess it will help with stuff?
ThatGuy 2026-03-18 7:26 p.m.
I'd be interested to see what you find. I don't know anything about that particular filter. I'd suggest testing by refreshing repeater stats, and recording the RSSI, Noise Floor and SNR. Perhaps do them side by side, one with filter and one without, and then compare. Try to keep other hardware like antennas, power, etc. the same.
ccw 2026-03-18 7:39 p.m.
The 1W raks have a filter built in already
7:41 p.m.
If you want to measure the attenuation in the filter you could just do it with a function generator and a scope. Just heads up don’t make your comparison with the 1W rack or a V4. The radios with an amplifier on the front end will just change their gain settings to achieve a target noise floor, so you won’t get anything meaningful from the numbers reported in the app
7:41 p.m.
(If I understand correctly)
ccw
If you want to measure the attenuation in the filter you could just do it with a function generator and a scope. Just heads up don’t make your comparison with the 1W rack or a V4. The radios with an amplifier on the front end will just change their gain settings to achieve a target noise floor, so you won’t get anything meaningful from the numbers reported in the app
curtisc83 2026-03-18 7:59 p.m.
Are the filters good?
ccw 2026-03-18 8:26 p.m.
Like you, I haven’t checked exhaustively, but as best I can tell, not bad. There’s the issue I was mentioning, namely that it’ll adjust the amplifier gain to keep the noise floor around -105 pretty much independent of what’s going on around you. If I drive up to a mystery transmitter that takes my V3 up to like -80 or worse and hold it there, the rak will get a momentary spike and go back to baseline and then a negative spike when I drive away, so there’s nothing to compare to directly without more effort, but my sense is that of the radios I have, the rak hears best
paul / BostonMesh 2026-03-18 9:20 p.m.
What’s the recommended firmware these days with the 1W booster? 1.14 or nightly-dev?
ccw 2026-03-18 9:21 p.m.
I’ve been using @weebl2000’s. Not sure how much of it has been merged yet
ccw
Like you, I haven’t checked exhaustively, but as best I can tell, not bad. There’s the issue I was mentioning, namely that it’ll adjust the amplifier gain to keep the noise floor around -105 pretty much independent of what’s going on around you. If I drive up to a mystery transmitter that takes my V3 up to like -80 or worse and hold it there, the rak will get a momentary spike and go back to baseline and then a negative spike when I drive away, so there’s nothing to compare to directly without more effort, but my sense is that of the radios I have, the rak hears best
curtisc83 2026-03-19 6:52 a.m.
I live in a city would a ceramic filter be beneficial since there is more RF floating around. From what you wrote I basically have two filters on my 1 watt raks now. I’m wondering is there a downside?
ccw 2026-03-19 10:01 a.m.
For an authoritative answer you want either an expert on these radios or experiment, but in principle, sure: among the downsides filters aren’t perfect, so every layer you add is throwing away signal too and costing transmit performance. My guess is if you try the built in filter and the background noise is still bad enough to cause you problems, you’d want to try a different strategy than just stacking up more filters of similar performance.
10:02 a.m.
They make filters with narrower pass bands. I haven’t tried one yet but folks seem to go for cavity filters
ccw
I’ve been using @weebl2000’s. Not sure how much of it has been merged yet
ThatGuy 2026-03-19 10:16 a.m.
I think almost everything we worked on on the 3401 has been merged. It's probably safe to go to the nightlies at this point.
ccw
For an authoritative answer you want either an expert on these radios or experiment, but in principle, sure: among the downsides filters aren’t perfect, so every layer you add is throwing away signal too and costing transmit performance. My guess is if you try the built in filter and the background noise is still bad enough to cause you problems, you’d want to try a different strategy than just stacking up more filters of similar performance.
curtisc83 2026-03-19 10:38 a.m.
This is really just me trying stuff out I actually don’t have issues. I figured mo filter mo cleaner signal mo better?
ThatGuy
I think almost everything we worked on on the 3401 has been merged. It's probably safe to go to the nightlies at this point.
ccw 2026-03-19 10:50 a.m.
Sweet
curtisc83
This is really just me trying stuff out I actually don’t have issues. I figured mo filter mo cleaner signal mo better?
ccw 2026-03-19 10:56 a.m.
I defer to the experts, and there are no doubt exceptions, but as a rule, no. Especially not if with every layer you’re adding connectors and cable and so forth. But no worries, because cheap as they are, the ceramic filters aren’t free, and for the price of a stack of them, you can just buy a cavity filter
ThatGuy 2026-03-19 1:48 p.m.
Since we have a quality group of testers in this thread, could I get y'all's help on testing this PR? It's to put a companion into OTA mode remotely for OTA updates, so you could use the Nordic app (other others) to update the firmware over BLE, without having to physically hit the button or plug up a cable. I think I have it working. I've tested on the RAK 4631 and the 3401, but I'd like to make sure the results are reproducable. Ultimately, there will be a button for it in the MC app. Until then, there is a python script that will trigger it. If you get a chance, try it out and let me know if it works for you. https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/pull/2078 (edited)
Add Remote DFU OTA update start to Companion firmware for nRF52- an...
Implementation of Feature Request/Issue #160 This PR is designed to allow putting the updated companion board into DFU OTA mode so that it can be updated over the air, using apps such as Nordic DFU...

ThatGuy 2026-03-23 5:37 p.m.
I'm not convinced that we have the transmit side of things working quite right on the 3401 boards. Is anyone else experiencing less than expected transmit performance? For example, we put a node up this weekend with 3401, powered by a solar panel that has 26000 mAh of battery, with a 12v to 5v converter that puts out up to 10A of power. Running to the 5v_EXT port with jumper set accordingly. So, power shouldn't be an issue. It's running a Diamond BC920 antenna. It's mouted at 190 feet up a tower. There was a 4631-based node about 90 feet lower on the tower, with a similar (if slightly inferior) antenna. I adverted from both and then looked at my next highest node that can hear that area. The 4631 advert was received at +4.25 dB. The 3401 was -4.50dB . Repeated and got the same results. Reception on the 3401 is outstanding however. Connect quality to the new node is a lot less reliable than the 4631-based node was. Anyone have and thoughts/guesses? (edited)
5:38 p.m.
That other node I measured from was running one of @weebl2000 's customs based off of v1.13. I tried doing a hot swap with another 3401 I had that was running v1.14. Got MUCH worse transmit performance there was well. Went back to the original hardware after a day.
Deverox 2026-03-23 6:08 p.m.
Interesting. I have 2 of these boards. One as a repeater in my window and one as my car node. They are replacing 2 HELTEC v4. I haven't had a good way to test them but ever since getting them set up I have been kind of disappointed with them. The first few days I felt like I wasn't getting as many messages out as I was with the HELTECs the all of the sudden they were doing great, then I would have a few days of lackluster performance. I do notice one of the repeaters near me some days I have an okish signal then some days it's -7.3. it's the reason I haven't finished building out the solar boxes and put them up on my roof. I keep hoping it just needs a few more firmware updates to get there but I'm losing hope. I'm thinking of getting a few 4631 boards and using those.
ThatGuy
That other node I measured from was running one of @weebl2000 's customs based off of v1.13. I tried doing a hot swap with another 3401 I had that was running v1.14. Got MUCH worse transmit performance there was well. Went back to the original hardware after a day.

9:35 p.m.

9:37 p.m.
Interesting enough on the companion side.. same board same firmware, 22 is the highest and what it was set to as default when flashed...
weebl2000 2026-03-23 10:21 p.m.
strange
10:22 p.m.
i think radiolib will reject 30 dBm anyway
10:22 p.m.
it'll be limited to 22 dBm regardless
10:22 p.m.
(at SX1262)
Aenigma
Thanks! Maybe RAK handles this in the 13302 module such that a setting of 22 will safely achieve the max output with the PA. I just never like pushing things to the limit and I was wondering if some of the overheating concerns were related to TX power settings, how the power is supplied to the kit (external vs internal), or something else (I saw some mentions of a buzzer circuit). (edited)
Aenigma 2026-03-23 10:46 p.m.
How does the Meshcore TX power setting compare to the actual SX1262 TX output power as an input to the SKY66122 amp?
Aenigma 2026-03-23 10:57 p.m.
I looked more closely at the schematics - there is a Pi attenuator between the SX1262 and the SKY66122 of about -20.6dB. When you add in the insertion loss of the SAW filter, setting the SX1262 to +22dBm puts about 0dBm into the SKY66122. Since the S21 (small signal gain) is about 33dB, that should get you up to the max +30dBm (1W) at the output at 5V. (edited)
weebl2000 2026-03-23 11:05 p.m.
Yeah meshcore just tells the SX1262 how much power to output
1
11:05 p.m.
It isn't output at the antenna
ccw
Has this been tested on the bench with something capable of directly measuring power output yet?
Aenigma 2026-03-23 11:18 p.m.
If folks don’t have a way to measure TX RF power, what is the current on the EX_5V input (assuming that’s how you are powering it) when transmitting? It might be hard to measure that too - a 100-byte packet at SF7 has a TX on time of less than 200ms…
ccw 2026-03-24 12:33 a.m.
I might be able to scrounge up a decent shunt resistor to measure that on a scope, but I think there are a couple folks around here with proper rf test equipment

ccw 2026-03-24 2:06 p.m.
So just to toss out a possibility: might be two problems here, one a consequence of the other. I’ve had one of these boards behaving very well for weeks on weebl’s variant 4 way up the thread, no overheating, apparently infinite battery life. Left it on the porch the other day and it got hot out there, like 130F air temp and idk how hot the board got. It locked up and was still >140 when it became responsive again. Lost a bunch of the nonvolatile memory contents, or at least its record of what was stored where. Anywho, point being: if there’s a bug overheating the MCU, lockups and other misbehavior may occur due to the heat itself
1
2:08 p.m.
Also might consider permanently stored settings suspect after overheat, which would explain performance issues cured by reflash
1
Deverox 2026-03-24 2:13 p.m.
Yeah I will definitely erase and reflash then. Hopefully that will help
ThatGuy 2026-03-24 3:45 p.m.
