Deverox 2026-03-25 8:16 a.m.
Hmm. Battery was showing 68%
Deverox 2026-03-25 8:37 a.m.
It's been plugged in all night too and the percentage still says 68%. Me change the battery and see if it starts charging.
ThatGuy 2026-03-25 10:01 a.m.
I typically use the 5V_EXT with the jumper set to external.
Deverox 2026-03-25 10:16 a.m.
Any good way to use USB C with the external? Any good 5v USB board I could wire up?
Deverox 2026-03-25 12:40 p.m.
So yeah it was a power issue... So on my broken one I noticed when I just had it plugged in with USB, in the meshcore app it said it was 0% battery. I figured that was normal since I thought USB didn't deliver enough power. It was just there for data and to charge if you had battery hooked up to it. Well I accidently hit the 0% and it showed me the voltage... It said 1.5v. I hooked up a new one with just USB attached in the app it always said 100% and 4.20v.. I must have shorted something and killed something on the board. It wasn't getting enough power and wasn't charging the battery either. I bet that's why I wasn't getting message out that far too.. I bet it wasn't transmitting anywhere near 1w.
Deverox 2026-03-25 1:13 p.m.
I have a support case open with RAK to see if I can get a replacement
andyshinn 2026-03-25 1:13 p.m.
the thing that makes it hard to troubleshoot is that the radio seems to work even if the amp doesn't get power. so, nearby it can seem like the radio is working even when it isn't really correctly powered
Deverox 2026-03-25 1:19 p.m.
Yeah. I can see it hit my repeater so I thought it was sorta working. It wasnt until I did some wardriving that I could see it wasn't getting very far at all. Especially in places it was working before.
Aenigma 2026-03-25 1:19 p.m.
Per the RAK information, the battery must be attached if you are using the USB port and have the jumper set to internal 5 V. The battery gets boosted to 5 V on the radio module, and the USB alone will not power the radio module.
Deverox 2026-03-25 1:22 p.m.
Yeah I know that, that's why I didn't bat an eye at first when I noticed the 0% battery and 2v current. But on a new board with just USB it shows 100% and 4.20v. the new board also is charging the batteries just fine now.
1:22 p.m.
Once I saw the new board said 4.20v I figured something was broken somewhere with power
Aenigma 2026-03-25 1:23 p.m.
Yeah it must be seeing just the 5 V from the USB and thinking that the battery is maxed out and reporting 100% and 4.2 V because that’s what it would expect a max battery to be
Deverox 2026-03-25 1:26 p.m.
Yup. Unfortunately I don't know enough about electrical engineering to really dig in and test every on the board to see what's going on. My best guess is I released some of the magic smoke and now magic board no worky anymore...
Aenigma 2026-03-25 1:37 p.m.
For your broken board, does the red charge light ever come on? Or was that the one where it would only be a dim red light? I suspect the charger IC is the problem. It’s not properly sourcing power from USB and delivering it to the battery terminals. That would explain why it would show 0% versus the one that does work showing 100% when no battery is attached. (edited)
Deverox 2026-03-25 1:52 p.m.
Yeah last night when I plugged it in, it was dim. I posted a pic.. as soon as I posted it it got brighter like normal.
1:52 p.m.
With no battery connected it stays bright
Aenigma 2026-03-25 1:53 p.m.
The one that works as expected, when you have no battery attached is the red light on with USB plug plugged in?
Deverox 2026-03-25 2:09 p.m.
Yes
Aenigma 2026-03-25 2:35 p.m.
Ok - so it must be that the output of the charger on the bad board isn’t delivering anything to the battery terminals. The unit still should be usable if it’s just that part on the base board - would need to handle battery charging separately/external.
ThatGuy 2026-03-25 4:24 p.m.
I flashed the dev firmware from @weebl2000 's builder last night. Ran cool all night...about 3-4 degrees above ambient.
4:25 p.m.
I'm going to try to see if the one with the modified pin does anything for me later this evening.
ThatGuy
I'm going to try to see if the one with the modified pin does anything for me later this evening.
Deverox 2026-03-25 4:35 p.m.
Good to know. I'm pretty sure my issue was self inflicted. I must have shorted it or something while messing with it so much the last few days. The spare board I had laying around got flashed with stock this morning and keys imported and it's working as it should now. Hitting the 2 or 3 repeaters in my area consistently. I'll do a drive test this evening if I have time to confirm. I don't think anything is wrong with my repeater since the flash back to stock since my heltec was hitting it consistently like normal yesterday.
ThatGuy 2026-03-25 6:00 p.m.
Yeah, it's just that you're not near the first person to experience overheating issues with this board.
6:03 p.m.
Which is what concerns me.
6:05 p.m.
*PART of what concerns me.
ccw 2026-03-25 6:08 p.m.
I generally power it off the external 5V connector and see some odd values reported. It likes 86% and 92% for some reason. I guess I don’t know exactly what to expect it to report when it doesn’t have anything plugged in to its battery terminals. Anyway, it’s been spending more time at 86% lately, so maybe something physical changing. Anywho, just a tangentially related observation
andyshinn 2026-03-25 6:09 p.m.
mine does that as well. it's the battery charge chip constantly pumping and trying to charge a battery that isn't there. it's basically always trying over and over again to charge
ThatGuy 2026-03-25 6:09 p.m.
Yeah, I noticed the same sort of thing. I'm running mine off a WaveShare regulated 5v into the EXT port, and it's calling 4.17v and some percentage I don't recall.
Deverox 2026-03-25 6:17 p.m.
This node is just a car node so if there is a way I can power it externally with USB that would be the best option so I don't have to have to battery on it. I just want it on when the car is on and off when I turn it off. Is there a USB board I could wire to the external connector? I just want to power it off the USB c car charger if possible. (edited)
ThatGuy
Yeah, I noticed the same sort of thing. I'm running mine off a WaveShare regulated 5v into the EXT port, and it's calling 4.17v and some percentage I don't recall.
ccw 2026-03-25 6:17 p.m.
Same setup, different value
ThatGuy 2026-03-25 6:18 p.m.
I'm curious why it doesn't say 5v
ccw 2026-03-25 6:19 p.m.
Presumably because it’s measuring on the battery side of the charging circuit?
6:20 p.m.
I wonder how it would behave with a small battery on there for a load. Is there any reason not to try that?
ThatGuy 2026-03-25 6:22 p.m.
Not that I know of. The initial RAK documentation suggested having a battery pretty much all the time.
6:22 p.m.
Of course, they've changed that.
Deverox
This node is just a car node so if there is a way I can power it externally with USB that would be the best option so I don't have to have to battery on it. I just want it on when the car is on and off when I turn it off. Is there a USB board I could wire to the external connector? I just want to power it off the USB c car charger if possible. (edited)
andyshinn 2026-03-25 7:28 p.m.
the problem is you need USB that can safely do >1.5A and sustain the 5V. if you are powering from your vehicle 12V supply then you want to get a USB charger that can do closer to 2A output. then you can cut one end of the USB cable and go into the EX_5V connector cable to do USB 5V -> EX_5V (set the jumper to EX_5V). you don't need anything else connected if you do EX_5V only
ccw 2026-03-25 9:03 p.m.
Won’t you only get half an amp that way? I thought you need at minimum an additional resistor to specify higher current
Aenigma 2026-03-25 9:15 p.m.
You can get USB PD cables that pre-negotiate the power. You would obviously need a USB PD charger for the car https://www.adafruit.com/product/5968
USB Type C 3.1 PD to 5.5mm Barrel Jack Cable - 5V 5A Output
This cable will let you get rid of all those wall adapters you have stashed in the plastic bin in the basement. Instead of multiple wall warts with different power and current capabilities, ...
9:17 p.m.
Add one of these and you don’t need to cut any wires https://www.adafruit.com/product/368
Female DC Power adapter - 2.1mm jack to screw terminal block
If you need to connect a DC power wall wart to a board that doesn't have a DC jack - this adapter will come in very handy! There is a 2.1mm DC jack on one end, and a screw terminal block ...
ccw 2026-03-25 9:42 p.m.
Nice, I like the cable. I’ve been using those little boards with a usb connector on one end, screw terminals on the other, and some DIP switches to pick the voltage
9:43 p.m.
Another way to do it without cutting anything up
👽 wazmac
I need to check if there is bus noise via the buck.
👽 wazmac 2026-03-25 10:32 p.m.
I put in a mini scope and the voltage is quite dirty. I had ordered different ones and they are good.
ccw 2026-03-25 10:34 p.m.
This is in reference to the external 5V?
ccw
This is in reference to the external 5V?
👽 wazmac 2026-03-25 10:39 p.m.
Yes. I just did another test with the better 5v board, it is a down regulator and it provides much cleaner source. I was doing some relative power output testing between the int_5v and ext_5v and knowing it is not accurate, can at least say that there is more output when powing from the external 5v.
10:43 p.m.
I used a NanoVNA with 30db input attenuation and set the thru mode on S21. With int_5v it was leveling out at tx 20. With the ext_5v it would increase slightly on tx 21 and tx 22. I know this is poor test, but it was enough to show there is a difference. I was using multiple adverts on the repeater at each setting and recording a rough average of the peaks.
ThatGuy 2026-03-25 10:45 p.m.
I’m working on getting my hands on a spectrum analyzer
ccw 2026-03-25 10:46 p.m.
Any evidence so far that the noise from the buck is making it through to the front end?
ccw
Any evidence so far that the noise from the buck is making it through to the front end?
👽 wazmac 2026-03-25 10:48 p.m.
I did not keep using the bad buck. Not worth it. I certainly don't have the equipment to do that test.
10:50 p.m.
I have ordered bits to convert a node build over to charging a 7.4v battery string, so I can use the 5v regulated board in situ. Then I will throw it back on the roof and see if it is any better with real traces.
10:51 p.m.
Horribly inefficient use of free sunlight. (edited)
ccw 2026-03-25 11:24 p.m.

thetooth 2026-03-25 11:31 p.m.

👽 wazmac 2026-03-26 2:40 a.m.
Double check you Ipex/ufl connector. I had one that looked ok, but under a big magnifying glass the inner connector had been bent down and was no longer contacting the centre pin. Very small air gap, but the symptom was the same. Could use locally but useless 200m down the road. (edited)
1
ccw 2026-03-26 3:13 a.m.
+1, this has happened to me too
👽 wazmac
Double check you Ipex/ufl connector. I had one that looked ok, but under a big magnifying glass the inner connector had been bent down and was no longer contacting the centre pin. Very small air gap, but the symptom was the same. Could use locally but useless 200m down the road. (edited)

ThatGuy 2026-03-26 11:40 a.m.
On one of my first 4631 boards, i put the strip antenna on it one time, and when I took it off to put on a real antenna pigtail, the whole connector came off the board. Rak basically told me to pound sand, and that that couldn't have happened. I assured them, it did. Still never got a replacement or my money back. I was unimpressed.
ccw 2026-03-26 11:42 a.m.
Lol, damn. To be fair, not sure I’d blame that one on the connector
ThatGuy 2026-03-26 11:43 a.m.

ccw 2026-03-26 11:43 a.m.

ThatGuy 2026-03-26 11:43 a.m.

11:44 a.m.

ccw 2026-03-26 11:44 a.m.

ccw 2026-03-26 11:44 a.m.
It’s really easy to get them misaligned end up mashing a bit
11:45 a.m.
Not that I’ve ever been impatient with one
ThatGuy 2026-03-26 11:46 a.m.
Yeah, I had the outside ring of one deform on me once. It split and was misshapen a bit. They should really move to a more robust connector.
ccw 2026-03-26 12:13 p.m.
They seem pretty committed to optimizing for cost and size. If someone made a beefed up model twice the size with more protection circuitry and robust connectors, I and at least a half dozen other people would be stoked to by them

Codes 2026-03-29 12:15 a.m.
I had a GPS plugged into my pocket node based on this radio and it drained the battery dead in 22 hours. Pulled the GPS and now I’m more like 5 days of battery life. I came in to ask if a particular slot is required for GPS to work on the companion FW?
Deverox 2026-03-29 12:34 a.m.
What version do you have? I think the 12500 works but the newer 12501 doesn't yet. I never could get it to work on a companion or repeater. It didn't drain my battery though.
12:34 a.m.
andyshinn 2026-03-29 11:57 a.m.
i'll try to find it. but yes there are slot conflicts with the GPS. i think slot D cannot be used but others are fine
Deverox
What version do you have? I think the 12500 works but the newer 12501 doesn't yet. I never could get it to work on a companion or repeater. It didn't drain my battery though.
Codes 2026-03-29 12:03 p.m.

paul / BostonMesh 2026-03-29 1:16 p.m.
I look forward to boards becoming more widely available. I’m convinced that there is an Rx issue with the 1W boards. When it is running as my primary repeater, I’m often having to re-send messages. Not all the time, but maybe 20% or so.
andyshinn 2026-03-29 1:17 p.m.
it's for sure more "deaf" than other non-1W boards. but i don't think it is as deaf as the 1W ebyte radios (unmodified) and Station G2
paul / BostonMesh 2026-03-29 1:23 p.m.
Funny enough, I have a station G2 as the alternate and have zero issues with it. I agree on the E22 though - absolutely not a fan.
1:23 p.m.
G2 definitely needs a cavity filter in high-noise environments.
andyshinn 2026-03-29 1:33 p.m.
i didn't have a G2 long enough to have a great opinion on it. just from what i've heard and seen in my area. cool device. but an underperformer compared to the hype and amount of docs and science behind it, unfortunately
Codes 2026-03-29 1:36 p.m.
My RAK 1W repeater greatly benefits from turning off rxgain in the CLI. It seems the RXgain built into the amp is overloading the Lora radio in some cases. I think that should probably be done on the companion firmware too, or at least pulled out as a preference.
1:38 p.m.
The deafness is far worse when then nodes are close to each other, I found putting my body between my companion and my repeater when they’re close greatly improved the lost Lora packets
andyshinn 2026-03-29 1:41 p.m.
i'll have to give that a try. i'm still waiting on a lot of PRs to bump to 1.14+ though. they just seem to hate my stuff i guess
Codes
My RAK 1W repeater greatly benefits from turning off rxgain in the CLI. It seems the RXgain built into the amp is overloading the Lora radio in some cases. I think that should probably be done on the companion firmware too, or at least pulled out as a preference.
Deverox 2026-03-29 1:59 p.m.
Funny you should say this. I was just wondering if I turned this off if my reception would be better. It's my main repeater too. Let me try this for a day or two and see how it goes.
Deverox 2026-03-29 2:27 p.m.

Codes
My RAK 1W repeater greatly benefits from turning off rxgain in the CLI. It seems the RXgain built into the amp is overloading the Lora radio in some cases. I think that should probably be done on the companion firmware too, or at least pulled out as a preference.
ccw 2026-03-29 3:02 p.m.
Rxgain is the analog amp and rxboost is the digital post-processing gain?
Fokai 2026-03-29 3:50 p.m.
Think it's very location specific environmentals for rxgain on/off. On works better at my location. Traces, packet % reporting on letsmesh, etc.
paul / BostonMesh 2026-03-29 4:42 p.m.
I went from dropping sporadic messages to no longer receiving any messages from the 10 neighbors. Rxgain is back on.
Not Sure 2026-03-29 5:02 p.m.
it was mentioned elsewhere that 1.14.1 upgrade may have defaulted or switched radio.rxgain to off by mistake. best to check it.

ThatGuy 2026-03-30 12:46 p.m.
I've had that happen on my mac. Does the same thing with the OTAFIX bootloader. No graceful exit. As soon as the uf2 file finishes, the board automatically powercycles with no shutdown or disconnect. It just vanishes. The mac is NOT a fan of that. (edited)
12:48 p.m.
There is a LOT of shared code between the two boards. In fact, when they first came out, the just said "use the 4631 setup" IIRC.

ThatGuy 2026-04-01 11:56 a.m.

ThatGuy
Can you post that test firmware? I've got a couple of suspect boards I want to examine.
Deverox 2026-04-01 11:56 a.m.
Use your serial app of choice. (screen gave me a werid stepping effect on output) I use tio. here was my command i used incase you need it. tio let me output it directly to a txt file at what ever ocation i wanted... tio /dev/cu.usbmodem3101 --map INLCRNL -L --log-file ~/Documents/RAK3401_HW_test.txt -t edit So also a heads up this seems to test TX during the test so please put an antenna on the baord before booting it with this firmware... looking at the logs it sends the full 22dbm of power (edited)
WB_HW_Test_RAK3401_1w_V2.0.0.uf2
414.5 KB
Deverox
Use your serial app of choice. (screen gave me a werid stepping effect on output) I use tio. here was my command i used incase you need it. tio let me output it directly to a txt file at what ever ocation i wanted... tio /dev/cu.usbmodem3101 --map INLCRNL -L --log-file ~/Documents/RAK3401_HW_test.txt -t edit So also a heads up this seems to test TX during the test so please put an antenna on the baord before booting it with this firmware... looking at the logs it sends the full 22dbm of power (edited)

ThatGuy 2026-04-01 12:17 p.m.
So, RAK now has the 3400/3401 chip available as a standalone product, and it comes with documentation. See: https://store.rakwireless.com/products/nrf52840-ble-core-module-wisblock?index=52 Significant discussion of the bootloader (and the fact that is uses the same as the 4631) here: https://docs.rakwireless.com/product-categories/wisblock/rak3401/quickstart/?utm_source=rak_store&utm_medium=organic&utm_campaign=rak3401_wisblock_ble_core_module_page&utm_term=quick_start_guide_for_rak3401_wisblock_ble_module&utm_content=documentation_link&_gl=1*hm4y0y*_gcl_au*MTg1MTA2MzU0NC4xNzc1MDU5Njg0

Low-power nRF52840 BLE core module with TCXO stability and modular WisBlock expansion. Ideal for battery or solar-powered IoT nodes.

Set up your RAK3401 BLE module with this quick start guide. Learn firmware flashing, low-power modes, and sensor integration.

Deverox 2026-04-01 11:14 p.m.
Rak saga update... They said the 3401 and booster modules look to be okay but the 2v is definitely an issue. USB only with no battery should read anywhere between 3.7v -4.2v. they want me to transplant both modules to one of my working rak19007 base boards and run both tests again. I'll try that in the morning as I don't feel like tearing down my meshtastic node out of the window tonight to test it. I hope it's just the 19007 base board. I feel they would replace that easier than sending me a whole new 1w kit. (edited)
Deverox 2026-04-02 9:56 a.m.

4:28 p.m.
im just posting my findings with my issues as i talk with RAK.
siliex 2026-04-02 11:21 p.m.
fwiw I've had a 1w booster up for 2 days now with zero issues - no obvious dropped incoming packets, no issues with tx at 30dbm, no obviously high battery drain or overheating. I wonder how many of the issues have been actual manufacturing problems vs firmware vs powering the unit the wrong way (which seems to be painfully easy to do by accident...) (edited)
Fokai 2026-04-03 12:02 a.m.
Mine has been up for 1 month continuously as an observer, no issues. I dunno… got it when it was first released, firmware wasn’t working back then.
bp-gone 2026-04-05 7:55 p.m.
I’m sure that there are some folks who got bad boards from RAK and others who transmitted without an antenna either by forgetting to connect one or by the connector popping off while buttoning things up. It happens. I’m also wondering if it’s possible that using the wrong firmware would result in overpowering the radio. I haven’t thought that one through.
7:59 p.m.
And then there are folks who haven’t really discovered the few proper ways to power it and jump to conclusions and we hear about the problem but don’t hear about the resolution. So my opinion is that these are fine. I have one. But we need to stop, read the docs (which are not great! Easy to misinterpret except for one clear part of one link) and also make sure we have antennas connected. There will always be a few boards that leave the factory bad and that happens as well.
Not Sure 2026-04-05 8:11 p.m.
The "22" max setting we see in software controls the SX1262 chip. A separate (but integrated in the package) amplifier bumps it to 30dBm in the 1W. So there shouldn't be any way overdrive it by software unless the 1262 has a fault. The same applies to 22 being 28dBm out on the Heltec v4. Think of ~22 as a scaling factor not a set number as it was before higher power chips came along.
8:11 p.m.
The docs were confusing when it first came out. I've noticed several revisions and re-wording. Much better now.
8:13 p.m.
...but people always find a way!
1
siliex 2026-04-05 9:13 p.m.
I'll join the train of some people probably forgetting to attach an antenna before turning it on, shit goes bad fast. I just burnt out mine because I stupidly located the power button in a spot that it could be pressed by opening the waterproof box for the repeater I was replacing the antenna on, so it was powered on with no antenna without me even noticing for a few minutes ;_;
siliex
I'll join the train of some people probably forgetting to attach an antenna before turning it on, shit goes bad fast. siliex 2026-04-05 11:31 p.m.

Codes 2026-04-06 12:17 a.m.
I’m trying to size a 5v regulator for this board and the 1.5a requirement is throwing me for a loop. The ones I’m finding can’t supply all those amps as the battery loses volts and so I think I have to upsize the boost converter to maintain the ability to supply 1.5a. Does that match others experience?

I just feel dumb because I very explicitly turned the power off on it, only to discover how dumb a location I chose to put a power switch I imagine this being 1w means it's probably easier to break too.

12:40 a.m.

Not Sure
what's wrong with the onboard one?
bp-gone 2026-04-06 12:42 a.m.
The RAK19007 charges at .4a max. Or at least earlier versions of it were limited to 400mA. So your solar could give more but it cannot accept it. So people use external solar charge controllers to maximize solar capture.
Lutin 2026-04-06 12:44 a.m.
I presume @Not Sure is talking about the onboard VBAT -> IN_5V converter
Not Sure 2026-04-06 12:44 a.m.
good to know
Lutin 2026-04-06 12:45 a.m.
which is an SGM66051-ADJ
12:45 a.m.
https://www.sg-micro.com/rect/assets/eda31bac-7dd0-47c6-8b39-d7f833382093/SGM66051.pdf
Not Sure 2026-04-06 12:47 a.m.
be interesting to see total energy consumption over 24 hours on a busy repeater.
Codes 2026-04-06 12:49 a.m.
Probably easiest to calculate a power budget based on the samples provided here than measure it for 24 hours
Not Sure
what's wrong with the onboard one?
Codes 2026-04-06 12:51 a.m.
The onboard 5v boost converter can’t supply enough current to keep the amp at the highest output as the battery voltage drops. It derates itself as the battery voltage drops
1
Lutin 2026-04-06 12:51 a.m.
yeah would be a bit complicated to get that on a repeater unless you just turn the charging off for 24 hours and go based off the battery's voltage/capacity curves
Codes
The onboard 5v boost converter can’t supply enough current to keep the amp at the highest output as the battery voltage drops. It derates itself as the battery voltage drops

yeah I lost the trace but I was seeing 3.8->3.3v voltage droop on a 2500mAh battery

Not Sure 2026-04-06 12:53 a.m.
we're gonna need a bigger battery!
Lutin 2026-04-06 12:53 a.m.
but the easiest way to fix that is to just make the battery bigger
12:54 a.m.
voltage drop is due to internal resistance so if you just make it a 1S2P you'll get half the drop, etc etc (edited)
Codes 2026-04-06 12:56 a.m.
Add bulk capacitance!
Lutin 2026-04-06 12:57 a.m.
ya just throw some supercaps on there too (edited)

12:58 a.m.
i feel like unless you already have a solid 5v power supply, you'd be better off trying to just handle the battery droop with more battery or some caps
2
1:00 a.m.
that SGM66051-ADJ is solid enough if you can just keep the supply voltage to it up
Codes 2026-04-06 1:02 a.m.

Aenigma 2026-04-06 4:14 p.m.
Glad to see folks getting real current/voltage/power data from this kit using the PPK2 - that’s a great tool for this sort of thing. Besides the TX power, it could help quantify sleep/idle/RX current versus FW features/versions. Since I’m new to MC, what’s the “typical” TX/RX duty cycle to be expected for a repeater? I realize that’s an impossible question but just curious if it is 1% or 10% or 25% etc.

5:00 p.m.

Lutin 2026-04-06 11:10 p.m.

👽 wazmac 2026-04-07 6:57 a.m.
I have just been testing with a local contact in Sydney and we have some interesting observations. We have clear LoS over 31km and a pair of RAK 1W repeaters. Between these two nodes we are getting consistent birdirectional SNR of 10.5db to 11db with relatively high noise floors of 84 and 94. The interesting thing though is that they do not seem not outstanding at all when communicating with other nodes. (edited)
Lbibass 2026-04-07 6:59 a.m.
I’m not shocked. Those noise floors aren’t great. Especially the 84 db one.
6:59 a.m.
Maybe see if you can get some additional filters on there?
👽 wazmac 2026-04-07 6:59 a.m.
This sort of echoes what others have said in regards to not seeming to be better than RAK 4361s. Could this be a case of a batch of dodgy clocks. Where these modules are just a tiny bit off and able to work with others, but excel when linked together. (edited)
7:01 a.m.
The above are on Aus Narrow 62.5 and these SNRs are the best we have seen over that distance (edited)
7:07 a.m.
TX is also turned down to meet regulatory constraints for narrow bandwidth. (edited)
👽 wazmac
This sort of echoes what others have said in regards to not seeming to be better than RAK 4361s. Could this be a case of a batch of dodgy clocks. Where these modules are just a tiny bit off and able to work with others, but excel when linked together. (edited)
ccw 2026-04-07 3:47 p.m.
Doesn’t this demonstrate just the opposite? If the 1Ws can talk to each other well, but they can’t talk to anything else, their rx has to be better than whatever the other radios are
Kevin 2026-04-07 4:27 p.m.
Do I need to turn the power up or just leave it at 22 and let the PA module handle the rest?
Lbibass 2026-04-07 4:44 p.m.
Leave it at 22

curtisc83 2026-04-07 7:21 p.m.
Ok how can I get my GPS working
7:22 p.m.
I just use the normal firmware
7:22 p.m.
I have the 12500 gps module on my 1 watt
7:24 p.m.

Kevin 2026-04-09 8:53 p.m.
I’m getting really horrible range. I’m powering it with USB right now. Is that acceptable? I have it set to external 5 V input
8:54 p.m.
When pinging my SNR “there” (to the repeater?) is a solid 11-12 and back (to my companion?) is like 0dB.
andyshinn 2026-04-09 8:55 p.m.
no, you need both USB + battery, USB + EX_5V, or just EX_5V at least 1.5A of power
8:55 p.m.
in that config you are likely getting no power to the 1W amplifier, it's just the signal without amplification
Kevin 2026-04-09 8:55 p.m.
Ah shoot, ok. So probably the PA is just being starved and that’s why I’m having issues
1
8:56 p.m.
Ok, well that’s an easy fix. Thanks!
1
bp-gone 2026-04-09 11:06 p.m.
EXT is for use with the special cable that connects to the booster. It needs 5V 1.5A. With the setting to INT, you have to have a battery and then either solar or USB.

bp-gone 2026-04-12 9:05 p.m.
The 12501 is L76K GPS, and the 12055 is a ublox GPS. But I think the Tag might use the L76k as well... both are supported generally by the GPS code, so I'm not sure if "it will just work" or if the variant files need more glue.
curtisc83
I would just want it for the time so the repeaters clocks wouldn’t drift
bp-gone 2026-04-12 9:06 p.m.

bp-gone 2026-04-12 9:19 p.m.
oh, by the way, in case you are interested. Today I tested out using supercapacitors (and a hybrid capacitor) on the battery connector to satisfy the "USB (or solar) plus battery" requirement for the INT setting. I use external solar charge controllers like the DFR0559 but it only does 1A and can't drive the RAK by itself. Put a supercap on the battery connector and it solves the problem.
1
9:20 p.m.
curtisc83 2026-04-12 9:20 p.m.

9:20 p.m.

bp-gone 2026-04-12 9:20 p.m.
the two supercaps are 15F, 5.5V. The green one is a Hybrid Supercap, 150F, costs $3 on Digikey.

ThatGuy 2026-04-16 6:19 p.m.
I think part of the problem is we don't (in my opinion) have this board fully stable yet. Early on, there were some people who were plugging add-on boards to it, and it was getting super hot and freaking out.
6:20 p.m.
I'm just trying to get a base config stable at this point.
bp-gone 2026-04-16 6:21 p.m.
Well, I had it working with BME, RTC, and a QWIIC breakout card in it (which wasn't populated with anything) and GPS and it was fine.
1
6:23 p.m.
And many many people don't understand the poorly worded power requirements and basically are running it wrong. Not victim blaming, the board has complicated power requirements that are simple when you understand them, but hard to decipher until you find the correct place in the docs.
6:24 p.m.
anyhow, I'm not convinced that this is a great node at this moment. We have great success using E22/E22P populated nodes like the ikoka family, and they don't pull over 1A on transmit, so I'm not quite sure why this thing balks at 1A of power input. Something is overengineered or underdelivered
ThatGuy 2026-04-16 6:26 p.m.
My problem is I get wildly varying results from board to board.
6:26 p.m.
And they've changed the tune on the power at least once, and maybe twice.
6:27 p.m.
I can't get a steady, reproduceable set of results, and I don't like inconsistency.
6:27 p.m.
If I'm putting nodes 200 feet up a tower, I need to know it's right.
6:29 p.m.
We had one installed and it appeared to work on the ground. Did as much testing a ground/roof edge level can do. Appeared to be OK. Ran it up the tower, and it has less than half the performance of a RAK4631 that was almost 100 feet lower.
6:29 p.m.
Needless to say, I was am quite frustrated. (edited)
Deverox 2026-04-16 11:19 p.m.
Yeah mine is still very hit and miss... Still haven't put it on the roof because of it. Some days it does fine.. others it can barely hit the repeater in the next neighborhood from me... No rhyme or reason. Sometimes I can send the same message 30 seconds later and it goes out like crazy to every repeater in a 5 mile radius. In my opinion it's not ready prime time yet... Just a test node for now
2
1
siliex 2026-04-16 11:50 p.m.
fwiw, aside from accidentally burning out the radio (which was user error, not the board's fault) I've only had good experiences with this board so far - just got my replacement today and put it in, working great. Using the internal power option with 4x 18650s and solar, no additional charge controller (yet? not sure if I'm going to get one). No additional modules on the board though I have an RTC on the way. Both boards I've run have had way better performance than the 4631 I was using in the exact same spot, though they also had a higher noise floor which is I guess expected. Weird how variable people's experiences are with this thing.
Deverox 2026-04-16 11:56 p.m.
To be fair this was with the same batch with the one I had issues with... I'll test more once I get the next batch in. I have also heard some people having amazing results with them so I'm hoping it's mostly just quality issues from the factory.
siliex 2026-04-16 11:58 p.m.
It does seem to be a combination of early units being bad, people not understanding power requirements, and maybe some addon modules causing issues. I guess it's hard to say for certain.
Not Sure 2026-04-17 1:16 a.m.
I've done one so far, powered off 5V ext only. Along with USB lines to PC for control. Split on one cable to the outdoor box. PC and 12V>5V converter just on the other side of wall, inside. So far so good. Just waiting for that gps clock function!
Not Sure
I've done one so far, powered off 5V ext only. Along with USB lines to PC for control. Split on one cable to the outdoor box. PC and 12V>5V converter just on the other side of wall, inside. So far so good. Just waiting for that gps clock function!

5:29 p.m.

Adafruit bq25185 USB / DC / Solar Charger with 5V Boost Board
We're always on the look out for better ways to make projects portable: being able to charge your battery in the most convenient manner will let projects run no matter what power is ...
5:29 p.m.
"The max draw from USB / DC / Solar is still 1A, if you need more current it will come from the battery and the chip can provide up to 3A current spikes from the battery to the load output"
5:31 p.m.
For the price and size, I think this is probably one of the best solar mppt/5v boost boards on the market for repeaters.
siliex 2026-04-17 6:32 p.m.

beala 2026-04-17 8:16 p.m.

8:17 p.m.

siliex 2026-04-17 8:17 p.m.
that looks like a different board?
8:18 p.m.
and that board does 4.5v, not 5v
8:18 p.m.
so neither of these boards fit the power requirements for the rak 1w
beala 2026-04-17 8:18 p.m.

siliex 2026-04-17 8:19 p.m.

my bad, it's from https://www.adafruit.com/product/6091, different product but same chip
siliex 2026-04-17 8:37 p.m.
yeah, so my concern on that is it's not 5v. Maybe it doesn't matter.
Kevin 2026-04-17 8:37 p.m.

8:39 p.m.

Kevin 2026-04-17 8:40 p.m.

beala 2026-04-17 9:09 p.m.
Hm seems complicated. The max current draw on the boost chip is a function of the inductor and Vin
ccw 2026-04-17 9:33 p.m.

beala 2026-04-17 9:37 p.m.

Kevin 2026-04-17 10:26 p.m.

ccw 2026-04-17 11:10 p.m.
If it does, I’d be curious how much a little extra buffer capacitance would help. Transmission doesn’t last long…
woketothetruth
Question for you, Sir. Mine is on the way. I was considering doing the same as you (if I understand your post above correctly). Did you have to modify your USB input cable so that you were not providing power to the RAK through the USB port AND your 12V to 5V buck converter? (edited)
Not Sure 2026-04-17 11:49 p.m.
Yes, I split a (somewhat) outdoor rated cable, took 5V+/- off for the EXT input and those go to the DC/DC inside. The two data lines and the same ground go to the PC. 5V didn't need to go to the USB but actually I don't remember if I fed both or not. I might have now that I think of it since i used a solderable USB male shoved into the RAK. It was just an easier place to land wires to vs janky wirenuts or crimps. But definitely no power from the USB port on the PC. I can externally and remotely switch the power on the 12V side since that comes from the ham radio battery/solar to force a hard reboot and such.
Not Sure 2026-04-18 12:10 a.m.
Have older arduino+relays at that site, but now using this https://www.amazon.com/dp/B0DNYYXQ3X here with ESPHome and HomeAssistant to switch 5V. It can also be powered from 5V as well as those other ranges. Only wifi but that's ok, in small doses.
2pcs AC/DC Power Supply ESP32 Development Board Programmable Develo...
2pcs AC/DC Power Supply ESP32 Development Board Programmable Development Board Wireless WiFi 4 Way Channel 5V Relay Module ESP32-WROOM-32E for Arduino AC 90-250V / DC 7-30V
ccw
If it does, I’d be curious how much a little extra buffer capacitance would help. Transmission doesn’t last long…
Kevin 2026-04-18 12:54 a.m.
I looked into that, I think you'd be in the realm of a supercap to be useful
12:54 a.m.
I'll definitely throw some normal sized caps at it though and see what happens
ccw 2026-04-18 1:36 a.m.
I haven’t ever used one in earnest, but since you mentioned it I took a cursory look, and it seems like you can get super caps with a suitable voltage rating, several F, couple hundred mOhm esr, and several A peak current for a few bucks. What’s the catch?
Kevin 2026-04-18 1:53 a.m.
I haven’t used them personally so not sure, but I’m sure there’s a catch
bp-gone 2026-04-18 11:05 a.m.
Scroll back (or I can find you a link) for a simple solution (when the battery is not used normally) of putting a supercap in the battery terminal.
11:06 a.m.
https://discord.com/channels/1343693475589263471/1462063989969256458/1493058041526882344
11:07 a.m.
The 15F supercap is overkill. And not expensive. The 150F hybrid supercap is 10x overkill and even cheaper. This allows power from a 1A 5V supply on the USB or Solar and the supercap deals with the spikes.
Kevin 2026-04-18 11:23 a.m.
Thanks @bp-gone !
ccw 2026-04-18 1:19 p.m.
So… it just works?
Kevin 2026-04-18 1:27 p.m.
I'm gonna order a couple and just put them on the 5v output of the Adafruit module to be safe.
bp-gone
oh, by the way, in case you are interested. Today I tested out using supercapacitors (and a hybrid capacitor) on the battery connector to satisfy the "USB (or solar) plus battery" requirement for the INT setting. I use external solar charge controllers like the DFR0559 but it only does 1A and can't drive the RAK by itself. Put a supercap on the battery connector and it solves the problem.
ThatGuy 2026-04-18 3:12 p.m.
@bp-gone Can you post links from where you ordered those?
Kevin 2026-04-18 3:18 p.m.
https://www.digikey.com/en/products/detail/cda-zhifengwei-technology/CSP-6R0L106R-TWX/22461649 https://www.digikey.com/en/products/detail/cda-zhifengwei-technology/CSP-6R0L256R-TW/22461663 I'm thinking the 25F one just to have plenty of margin, plus it has lower ESR.
bp-gone 2026-04-18 4:01 p.m.
If you do some math, the 15F one will run continuous 1.5A for quite a while. You really don’t need that much but it’s your choice!
4:01 p.m.
And yes I got them on Digikey
4:03 p.m.
https://www.digikey.com/en/products/detail/kyocera-avx/SCMU33F156SRBA0/12664022
4:03 p.m.
https://www.digikey.com/en/products/detail/ymin/SLD4R2L1571030/28244028
4:05 p.m.
Remember it’s only drawing over 1A during transmit. Even at an overloaded 50% transmit airtime that means you don’t need a lot of capacitance with the main power supplying it
4:06 p.m.
If it draws evenly from mains and battery, it’s drawing less than 0.75A for a brief time from the battery. 15F is plenty.
bp-gone 2026-04-18 6:16 p.m.
Oh and you need to put in your own pigtail. Another Austinite showed me something that came with a pigtail pre installed. Hold on
6:19 p.m.
https://www.digikey.com/en/products/detail/tecate-group/SCAP-PBLL-15-0-5-4/9929727

Well let's see if this new firmware helps or hurts.. https://blog.meshcore.io/2026/04/19/release-1-15-0

3:57 p.m.
I confirmed it works with MC fw (unlike gps) https://store.rakwireless.com/products/rtc-module-rak12002
RTC Module Micro Crystal RV-3028-C7 | RAK12002
RAK12002 is a WisBlock Real-Time Clock (RTC) module featuring Micro Crystal RV-3028-C7 with battery backup. Maintains date & time even during power loss, syncing with WisBlock Core upon power restoration.

ccw 2026-04-20 1:57 a.m.

ThatGuy 2026-04-20 5:54 p.m.
For what it is worth, I was finally able to lay my hands on a TinySA to test one of my RAK 1W kits that wasn't working as expected. As I suspected, it's a burned out transmitter section. Tried it on multiple versions of the firmware, and in all possible power configs. Maximum transmit I could get out of it was -35 db. I compared it with a new-in-package 1W, which showed +29.5 db or so using the same testing setup and powered solely through the EXT5V connector with appropriate jumper selection. While I was at it, I went ahead and tested the new working board with the jumper set to INT5V, and using only battery and then only USB, and it had underwhelming transmit performance on both...in the range of -10db. Powering with both Battery and USB transmitted at +29.4 db or so, as designed. So, they aren't kidding about needing to use both sources simultaneously if you're powering from the main board and not using the EXT_5V connector. If you decide to replicate this testing yourself, please note that you **__need** a 40db or so signal attenuator to avoid burning out the front end on the TinySA, which is rated apparently up to about 10db. 1W = roughly 30db, so the 40 attenuator gives you plenty of room. You just have to do the math at the end. (edited)
1
siliex 2026-04-20 7:09 p.m.
I'm curious if you could replace just the fried 13302 board since they sell those separately, bit cheaper than replacing the whole kit. I imagine it'd work, the 13302 contains the radio and in theory nothing else should be fried... (edited)
7:10 p.m.

deej

ThatGuy 2026-04-20 9:16 p.m.
Sure. Give me a bit and I’ll write it up.

ThatGuy 2026-04-20 9:17 p.m.
I asked this question today in an email to RAK tech support. Will report back when I hear from them.
siliex

ccw 2026-04-20 10:00 p.m.
Well, that’s pretty unambiguous. I’m blind I guess
ThatGuy 2026-04-21 1:34 a.m.
TinySA setup: I used a TinySA Ultra+. Calibrate the unit according to the manufacturer’s instructions. After this, hook up as shown below. Tap the Screen. Hit Frequency. I set up the frequency range on the device to scan from 910.40 to 911.60, since I use 910.525 as my center frequency. This keeps the scanning path short and makes it less likely you’ll miss the transmission peak. Back out to the main menu, then Tap “Trace”. If it isn’t already selected, pick a trace you want to use. I used Trace 1. Click “Enable”. Then click “Calc Off”. Pick “Max Hold”. This will cause it to lock the highest peak on the screen so you can read it. After you’ve taken a measurement, and want to do another, tap the screen again, and hit “Max Hold” again. This will reset the pointer peak. You can do this over and over as you take measurements.
1
1:35 a.m.
1:36 a.m.
ThatGuy
TinySA setup: I used a TinySA Ultra+. Calibrate the unit according to the manufacturer’s instructions. After this, hook up as shown below. Tap the Screen. Hit Frequency. I set up the frequency range on the device to scan from 910.40 to 911.60, since I use 910.525 as my center frequency. This keeps the scanning path short and makes it less likely you’ll miss the transmission peak. Back out to the main menu, then Tap “Trace”. If it isn’t already selected, pick a trace you want to use. I used Trace 1. Click “Enable”. Then click “Calc Off”. Pick “Max Hold”. This will cause it to lock the highest peak on the screen so you can read it. After you’ve taken a measurement, and want to do another, tap the screen again, and hit “Max Hold” again. This will reset the pointer peak. You can do this over and over as you take measurements.

ThatGuy
For what it is worth, I was finally able to lay my hands on a TinySA to test one of my RAK 1W kits that wasn't working as expected. As I suspected, it's a burned out transmitter section. Tried it on multiple versions of the firmware, and in all possible power configs. Maximum transmit I could get out of it was -35 db. I compared it with a new-in-package 1W, which showed +29.5 db or so using the same testing setup and powered solely through the EXT5V connector with appropriate jumper selection. While I was at it, I went ahead and tested the new working board with the jumper set to INT5V, and using only battery and then only USB, and it had underwhelming transmit performance on both...in the range of -10db. Powering with both Battery and USB transmitted at +29.4 db or so, as designed. So, they aren't kidding about needing to use both sources simultaneously if you're powering from the main board and not using the EXT_5V connector. If you decide to replicate this testing yourself, please note that you **__need** a 40db or so signal attenuator to avoid burning out the front end on the TinySA, which is rated apparently up to about 10db. 1W = roughly 30db, so the 40 attenuator gives you plenty of room. You just have to do the math at the end. (edited)
beala 2026-04-21 9:16 a.m.
Wait so powering from a battery only (connected to the base board with INT_5V) also resulted in less output? That's surprising to me! This was a fully charged 18650 li-ion?
👽 wazmac
Mine is a NanoVNA H4 (edited)
ThatGuy 2026-04-21 9:21 a.m.
I was told a VNA wouldn't do it. Has to be a SA. I have a NanoVNA H4 as well, but a couple of folks on here, plus AI, both indicated a TinySA was the way to go. A friend got one, and I borrowed it.
beala
Wait so powering from a battery only (connected to the base board with INT_5V) also resulted in less output? That's surprising to me! This was a fully charged 18650 li-ion?
ThatGuy 2026-04-21 9:26 a.m.
Yes, at least as far as I can tell. It seems RAK has changed the wording on the docs yet again, but previously, it said you had to do either 5V EXT for everything, or Battery + USB or Battery + a 5v source into the Solar Port. I don't understand why 5v into the USB port wouldn't do it alone, but earlier versions of the docs said it wouldn't, and my testing supports that. I plugged up USB, forgot the battery for a moment, and switched it over to INT, and was getting lower than expected transmit. Then, I was like, 'oh yeah, the battery', plugged it in, and it jumped up to 30db immediately. I don't have the known good board here anymore. I sent it out for deployment today, so I can't reverify immediately. Once I lay hands back on a working 1W, I'll verify this result again.
ThatGuy
Yes, at least as far as I can tell. It seems RAK has changed the wording on the docs yet again, but previously, it said you had to do either 5V EXT for everything, or Battery + USB or Battery + a 5v source into the Solar Port. I don't understand why 5v into the USB port wouldn't do it alone, but earlier versions of the docs said it wouldn't, and my testing supports that. I plugged up USB, forgot the battery for a moment, and switched it over to INT, and was getting lower than expected transmit. Then, I was like, 'oh yeah, the battery', plugged it in, and it jumped up to 30db immediately. I don't have the known good board here anymore. I sent it out for deployment today, so I can't reverify immediately. Once I lay hands back on a working 1W, I'll verify this result again.

If it cannot be powered off a battery only, then that implies you're getting worse transmit at night, so would be grateful if you could confirm or not.
ThatGuy 2026-04-21 9:37 a.m.
Thinking back on it, I THINK I tested battery only and also got lower performance, but I can't say 100% for certain. I probably would have tested that variation. Based on the docs, I would expect battery only isn't sufficient. I'll follow up once I get a working board back in my hands.
ThatGuy 2026-04-21 9:51 a.m.
I think this board's very persnickety power requirements, along with multiple sets of support doc revisions, have caused a lot of issues for people.

TinySA setup: I used a TinySA Ultra+. Calibrate the unit according to the manufacturer’s instructions. After this, hook up as shown below. Tap the Screen. Hit Frequency. I set up the frequency range on the device to scan from 910.40 to 911.60, since I use 910.525 as my center frequency. This keeps the scanning path short and makes it less likely you’ll miss the transmission peak. Back out to the main menu, then Tap “Trace”. If it isn’t already selected, pick a trace you want to use. I used Trace 1. Click “Enable”. Then click “Calc Off”. Pick “Max Hold”. This will cause it to lock the highest peak on the screen so you can read it. After you’ve taken a measurement, and want to do another, tap the screen again, and hit “Max Hold” again. This will reset the pointer peak. You can do this over and over as you take measurements.
deej 2026-04-21 9:55 a.m.
Thank you! What I had done was after doing the self test and calibration, I set the freq from 900 to 920, then on the TinySA, Level, Gain, -30 (for an attached 30 dB attenuator), then Level, Trigger, Level set to -40, then Level, Trigger, Single, and then hit Advert on the node. This mostly worked, but on a 22 dB radio I am seeing around 18 dB, and on the RAK 30 dB I was seeing about 26 dB. I'll give your method a try.
👽 wazmac
I have used this method before but did not see the “Calc Off” and Pick “Max Hold” are they always available or only in certain context?
deej 2026-04-21 9:56 a.m.
From the main menu on the TinySA, click Trace, then Calc Off, and it will bring up another menu with the Max Hold option.
1
deej
Thank you! What I had done was after doing the self test and calibration, I set the freq from 900 to 920, then on the TinySA, Level, Gain, -30 (for an attached 30 dB attenuator), then Level, Trigger, Level set to -40, then Level, Trigger, Single, and then hit Advert on the node. This mostly worked, but on a 22 dB radio I am seeing around 18 dB, and on the RAK 30 dB I was seeing about 26 dB. I'll give your method a try.

Just realized I can pull my 1W repeater down out of the tree and test with it. I’ll do that next time I’m at the house.

siliex 2026-04-21 11:29 a.m.
On a separate note, I've got a cavity filter with my repeater's name on it. Going to try to throw it on some time this week, see how it improves the noise floor since it's definitely way worse on the 1w than the 4631 that used to be there. The 1w is getting a crazy number of received packet errors - about 2x the rate that the old repeater did so I wonder if it'll improve things.
1
siliex
On a separate note, I've got a cavity filter with my repeater's name on it. Going to try to throw it on some time this week, see how it improves the noise floor since it's definitely way worse on the 1w than the 4631 that used to be there. The 1w is getting a crazy number of received packet errors - about 2x the rate that the old repeater did so I wonder if it'll improve things.
ThatGuy 2026-04-21 12:01 p.m.
Will be very curious to see your results. I've noticed the increased packet errors on mine as well.
ccw 2026-04-21 12:53 p.m.
I’m surprised to hear this. In an entirely non-quantitative way I feel like the 1W is the best receiver I have by a country mile
beala 2026-04-21 12:55 p.m.
I've heard both that the 13302 is: (a) the best receiver they've used. the only one that can get them connected to the mesh and (b) total trash
12:56 p.m.
My personal experience is that it's working quite well for me afaict
ccw 2026-04-21 12:56 p.m.
Seems like it also ought to be better than an unfiltered 4631 since it’s already got a built in filter. Whether it’s the best ever or not, ought to be better than nothing. Any chance the packet error fraction is confusing things because the denominator is going up at the same time? ie seeing a bunch of packets the 4631 would have missed entirely?
siliex 2026-04-21 1:34 p.m.
It's worked fine for me, but the noise floor is anywhere from 10-30db higher than the previous 4631. If it's just "hearing" better then that may be why, but all the more reason to try a proper cavity filter to see if it can hear even further.
Not Sure 2026-04-21 1:54 p.m.
Only have one 1W as a repeater: -104dB noise. 18% errors. Naked, no filters. Surrounded by RF trash apartments/condos on three sides. (WIfi doesn't work two rooms over is how noisy it is.). Heltec v4 with saw filter with two quiet neighbor houses an acre away has a higher noise floor and errors. Can't really compare reported noise floor values between models, hw is different and sw just reports whatever the radio spits out. It's not calibrated and then we have LNAs before/after test points messing things up. So i guess my result is: it works fine. (edited)
1:56 p.m.
I think ppl can't follow the instructions on how to power it are having the issues. It's pretty simple. Ext 5V with proper power supply, or a good battery+something to charge it. Not the cheapest battery you can find with a dodgy twisted together connection.
1
Not Sure
I think ppl can't follow the instructions on how to power it are having the issues. It's pretty simple. Ext 5V with proper power supply, or a good battery+something to charge it. Not the cheapest battery you can find with a dodgy twisted together connection.
ccw 2026-04-21 4:31 p.m.
That’s what I thought too, until @ThatGuy reported measurement to the contrary.

It's worked fine for me, but the noise floor is anywhere from 10-30db higher than the previous 4631. If it's just "hearing" better then that may be why, but all the more reason to try a proper cavity filter to see if it can hear even further.
ccw 2026-04-21 4:32 p.m.
Seems like reported noise floor is is meaningless once automatic gain control is involved
4:32 p.m.

beala
I've heard both that the 13302 is: (a) the best receiver they've used. the only one that can get them connected to the mesh and (b) total trash
ThatGuy 2026-04-21 4:41 p.m.
I can confirm that I have said both of these, occasionally simultaneously.
siliex 2026-04-21 4:41 p.m.
Maybe. The increased error rate is worrying though - it used to be about half the number of received packets, now it's almost equal (so 50% of what it sees is unreadable, from what I understand). Whatever it is, I'll see what the cavity filter does once I get the extra cables I need for it (had the wrong ones... oops).
Codes 2026-04-21 7:20 p.m.
For what it's worth, I am running solar 2 repeaters and my pocket node on the internal 5v regulator and a honkin' battery. We did a TX power test this weekend with a fully charged 3000mah pouch cell and showed 1.2W, as expected. didn't matter if we had USB plugged in or not for the battery nearly fully charged. We didn't try other battery charge states, or smaller batteries. All my nodes are doing really well in terms of power output as far as I can tell, but they all also have huge batteries. 3000mah for my pocket node, 8000mah for one repeater, and at least 15,000mah for the other. (edited)
7:23 p.m.
The RX is dicy, I have one that is nearly deaf because of cell towers nearby, and another that is totally happy on the top of a rooftop with no additional filtering. I do notice that my pocket node noise floor is significantly higher than my non-amped node. I'm putting a cavity filter on the cell towers site, hoping that will improve things a lot
7:25 p.m.
My pocket node with the same antenna and feedline as the unamped node with stock FW 14.1 hears significantly more things than the stock radio, routeinly is able to pull things out of the noise floor that the other pocket node can't. The only radio I have that out performs it in RX in reasonable environments is the T1000e (edited)
7:26 p.m.
But there's that whole, deafness problem in noisy environments, it is worse than an unamped node, but of course it is, we all knew that LNAs are trouble in those environments.
7:29 p.m.
Overall, I'm really happy with them, for simple situations they are drop in like the unamped nodes. For more challenging environments they need some more care and feeding, but that's just how amped nodes are, there's no free lunch.
siliex 2026-04-21 7:30 p.m.
Exactly why I'm testing a cavity filter to see how it affects things. Was going to today, but accidentally broke the enclosure for the repeater while trying to figure out where to mount the filter so it'll be going back up tomorrow once I have a new one... my experience with this device has been a comedy of errors all of my own making lmao

ThatGuy 2026-04-21 8:33 p.m.
Here’s what I found. Add +40 to the displayed values to account for the attenuator. USB only is the only method that didn’t work at full power. (edited)
2
8:35 p.m.
I think the reason is a cheap usb chip that can’t deliver 1.5amps. But I’m not certain of that.
ccw 2026-04-21 8:37 p.m.
Ok, fantastic. I think consensus is restored, then
ThatGuy
Here’s what I found. Add +40 to the displayed values to account for the attenuator. USB only is the only method that didn’t work at full power. (edited)
siliex 2026-04-21 10:12 p.m.
Which matches what they say, nice. Thanks for confirming!
10:15 p.m.
I assume that because the int power option is coming from a modular baseboard that isn't only for this radio, so they just didn't bother adding something more.
10:15 p.m.
since USB will work just fine for basically anything else you'd put on that board
siliex
Which matches what they say, nice. Thanks for confirming!
ThatGuy 2026-04-21 11:00 p.m.
Happy to help
11:01 p.m.
What I find odd is that it specifically says you can't power it from the 3.3v rail alone, which is what I thought the battery essentially was doing.
1
Aenigma 2026-04-21 11:58 p.m.
For those wondering about the power paths, if you look at the schematics, the SKY66122 FEM only gets powered from either the EX_5V input OR the 5V boost converter which takes the battery voltage as its input. The USB VBUS is NOT connected to the amp, that’s why it won’t work properly with just USB input. (edited)
1
1
Codes 2026-04-21 11:58 p.m.
I haven't looked at the schematic, but from what I understand, the board doesn't do any USB-PD triggering to allow the supply to provide more amps or volts on the USB. The 190007 can only pull 450ma from USB to charge the battery, so 450ma on the battery rail is just not enough to run the 5v booster
Codes
I haven't looked at the schematic, but from what I understand, the board doesn't do any USB-PD triggering to allow the supply to provide more amps or volts on the USB. The 190007 can only pull 450ma from USB to charge the battery, so 450ma on the battery rail is just not enough to run the 5v booster
ThatGuy 2026-04-22 12:00 a.m.
I concur. It's likely a low-amperage USB-C implementation. Probably tops out at 750ma or so would be my guess. That's enough to charge the battery and still operate the board. (edited)
ThatGuy
I concur. It's likely a low-amperage USB-C implementation. Probably tops out at 750ma or so would be my guess. That's enough to charge the battery and still operate the board. (edited)
Aenigma 2026-04-22 12:05 a.m.
Only 350mA https://docs.rakwireless.com/product-categories/wisblock/rak19007/datasheet/#battery-connector-1

siliex 2026-04-22 7:49 p.m.
Seems more in line with what I was seeing on the 4631. Packets received is 1423, packet errors is 1001. I think total packets received does not include errors? So total seen would be 2424 (rx + errors). Not entirely sure how the stat works. Checked the code, my assumption is correct. When I checked it before adding the filter it was around 14k rx and 14k errors from a few days of uptime. Will keep the thread updated over the next few days in case anything changes. (edited)

Deverox 2026-04-22 9:10 p.m.
The replacement RAK 3401 got here today... Installed it and it was working fine for all of 10 minutes. Flashed the newest meshcore Repeater firmware and in the middle of setting it up it locked up on me. Had to reboot it. It locked back up a few minutes later. Never could finish getting it set up. Noticed it was very warm to the touch. Reflashed the test firmware they had me use during testing with tech support... It's showing 2v again... I think this board freid the new module... Sending them an email back...
